Schema migration system for TaskStore backends.\n//!\n//! Migrations are numbered SQL files embedded in the binary at compile time.\n//! Each migration is applied exactly once, in order, and tracked in the\n//! schema_versions table (SQLite) or schema_version key (Redis).\n//!\n//! ## Version checking\n//!\n//! - Binary version = max migration number compiled into the binary\n//! - Store version = max migration applied to the store\n//! - On startup: if store version > binary version, refuse to start with\n//!   MiroirError::SchemaVersionAhead — operator must restore from backup or\n//!   upgrade the binary.\n//! - If binary version > store version, apply pending migrations.\n\nuse crate::Result;\nuse std::collections::BTreeMap;\n\n/// A single migration with its version number and SQL content.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct Migration {\n    /// Monotonic version number (e.g., 1 for 001_initial.sql)\n    pub version: i64,\n    /// Raw SQL to execute for this migration\n    pub sql: &'static str,\n}\n\n/// Registry of all available migrations compiled into the binary.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct MigrationRegistry {\n    /// Ordered map of version -> migration\n    migrations: BTreeMap<i64, Migration>,\n}\n\nimpl MigrationRegistry {\n    /// Create a new registry from a list of migrations.\n    ///\n    /// Panics if migration versions are not unique.\n    pub fn new(migrations: &[Migration]) -> Self {\n        let mut map = BTreeMap::new();\n        for m in migrations {\n            if map.insert(m.version, m.clone()).is_some() {\n                panic!(\"duplicate migration version: {}\", m.version);\n            }\n        }\n        Self { migrations: map }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the maximum migration version (the binary's schema version).\n    pub fn max_version(&self) -> i64 {\n        self.migrations.keys().last().copied().unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n\n    /// Get all migrations that need to be applied to reach the target version.\n    ///\n    /// Returns migrations with version > `current_version` in ascending order.\n    pub fn pending_migrations(&self, current_version: i64) -> Vec<&Migration> {\n        self.migrations\n            .values()\n            .filter(|m| m.version > current_version)\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Validate that the store version is not ahead of the binary version.\n    ///\n    /// Returns an error if store_version > binary_version.\n    pub fn validate_version(&self, store_version: i64) -> Result<()> {\n        let binary_version = self.max_version();\n        if store_version > binary_version {\n            return Err(crate::MiroirError::SchemaVersionAhead {\n                store_version,\n                binary_version,\n            });\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Build the migration registry from embedded migration files.\n///\n/// This function is generated by the macro that embeds migration files.\n/// For now, we manually construct the registry with the available migrations.\npub fn build_registry() -> MigrationRegistry {\n    MigrationRegistry::new(&[\n        Migration {\n            version: 1,\n            sql: include_str!(\"../migrations/001_initial.sql\"),\n        },\n        Migration {\n            version: 2,\n            sql: include_str!(\"../migrations/002_feature_tables.sql\"),\n        },\n        Migration {\n            version: 3,\n            sql: include_str!(\"../migrations/003_task_registry_fields.sql\"),\n        },\n        Migration {\n            version: 4,\n            sql: include_str!(\"../migrations/004_mode_b_operations.sql\"),\n        },\n        Migration {\n            version: 5,\n            sql: include_str!(\"../migrations/005_jobs_chunking.sql\"),\n        },\n    ])\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_registry_max_version() {\n        let registry = MigrationRegistry::new(&[\n            Migration {\n                version: 1,\n                sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n            },\n            Migration {\n                version: 2,\n                sql: \"ALTER TABLE foo ADD COLUMN name TEXT;\",\n            },\n        ]);\n\n        assert_eq!(registry.max_version(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_pending_migrations() {\n        let registry = MigrationRegistry::new(&[\n            Migration {\n                version: 1,\n                sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n            },\n            Migration {\n                version: 2,\n                sql: \"ALTER TABLE foo ADD COLUMN name TEXT;\",\n            },\n            Migration {\n                version: 3,\n                sql: \"CREATE INDEX idx_foo_name ON foo(name);\",\n            },\n        ]);\n\n        // No pending migrations when at max version\n        assert!(registry.pending_migrations(3).is_empty());\n\n        // All migrations pending when at version 0\n        let pending = registry.pending_migrations(0);\n        assert_eq!(pending.len(), 3);\n        assert_eq!(pending[0].version, 1);\n        assert_eq!(pending[1].version, 2);\n        assert_eq!(pending[2].version, 3);\n\n        // Only migrations 2 and 3 pending when at version 1\n        let pending = registry.pending_migrations(1);\n        assert_eq!(pending.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(pending[0].version, 2);\n        assert_eq!(pending[1].version, 3);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_version_success() {\n        let registry = MigrationRegistry::new(&[Migration {\n            version: 5,\n            sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n        }]);\n\n        // Store at same version is OK\n        registry.validate_version(5).unwrap();\n\n        // Store behind binary is OK (will apply migrations)\n        registry.validate_version(3).unwrap();\n\n        // Fresh store (version 0) is OK\n        registry.validate_version(0).unwrap();\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_version_store_ahead() {\n        let registry = MigrationRegistry::new(&[Migration {\n            version: 3,\n            sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n        }]);\n\n        // Store ahead of binary should fail\n        let result = registry.validate_version(5);\n        assert!(result.is_err());\n        match result.unwrap_err() {\n            crate::MiroirError::SchemaVersionAhead {\n                store_version,\n                binary_version,\n            } => {\n                assert_eq!(store_version, 5);\n                assert_eq!(binary_version, 3);\n            }\n            _ => panic!(\"expected SchemaVersionAhead error\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    #[should_panic(expected = \"duplicate migration version\")]\n    fn test_duplicate_version_panics() {\n        MigrationRegistry::new(&[\n            Migration {\n                version: 1,\n                sql: \"CREATE TABLE foo (id INT);\",\n            },\n            Migration {\n                version: 1,\n                sql: \"CREATE TABLE bar (id INT);\",\n            },\n        ]);\n    }\n}\n","numLines":216,"startLine":1,"totalLines":216}}}

}\n120\t\n121\t    /// Start a streaming dump import.\n122\t    pub async fn start_import(\n123\t        &self,\n124\t        index_uid: String,\n125\t        dump_data: Vec<u8>,\n126\t {\n200\t        // Update phase to reading\n201\t        Self::update_phase(&imports, import_id, DumpImportPhase::Reading).await;\n202\t\n203\t        // Parse NDJSON and route documents\n204\t // Extract primary key value\n223\t            let pk_value = doc\n224\t                .get(&primary_key)\n225\t                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n226\t                .ok_or_else(|| {\n227\t                    MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n228\t                        \"missing or invalid primary key field: {}\",\n229\t                        primary_key\n230\t                    ))\n231\t                })?;\n232\t\n233\t            // Compute shard and route\n234\t            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n235\t\n236\t            // Get target nodes for this shard (assign across all replica groups)\n237\t            let target_nodes: Vec<NodeId> = topology\n238\t                .groups()\n239\t                .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n240\t                .collect();\n241\t\n242\t            if target_nodes.is_empty() {\n243\t                return .push(doc.clone());\n252\t            }\n253\t\n254\t            processed += 1;\n255\t\n256\t            // Flush buffers when they reach batch size\n257\t            if processed % config.batch_size as u64 == 0 {\n258\t                Self::flush_buffers(\n259\t                    &index_uid,\n260\t                    &mut per_target_buffers,\n261\t                    &config,\n262\t                    &imports,\n263\t                    import_id,\n264\t                    processed,\n265\t                )\n266\t                .await?;\n267\t            }\n268\t        }\n269\t\n270\t        // Final flush\n271\t        Self::flush_buffers(\n272\t            &index_uid,\n273\t            &mut per_target_buffers,\n274\t            &config,\n275\t            &imports,\n276\t            import_id,\n277\t            processed,\n278\t        )\n279\t        .await?;\n280\t\n281\t        // Mark complete\n282\t        Self::update_phase(&imports, import_id, Streaming routed dump import (plan §13.9).\n//!\n//! Intercepts dump imports and routes each document to the correct shard\n//! instead of broadcasting to all nodes.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::router::{shard_for_key, assign_shard_in_group};\nuse crate::topology::{Topology, NodeId};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Dump import configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DumpImportConfig {\n    /// Import mode: \"streaming\" or \"broadcast\".\n    #[serde(default = \"default_mode\")]\n    pub mode: String,\n    /// Batch size for per-target POSTs.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_batch_size\")]\n    pub batch_size: u32,\n    /// Parallel target writes.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_parallel\")]\n    pub parallel_target_writes: u32,\n    /// Memory buffer cap (bytes).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_memory_buffer\")]\n    pub memory_buffer_bytes: u64,\n    /// Chunk size for Mode C coordinator.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_chunk_size\")]\n    pub chunk_size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\nfn default_mode() -> String {\n    \"streaming\".into()\n}\nfn default_batch_size() -> u32 {\n    1000\n}\nfn default_parallel() -> u32 {\n    8\n}\nfn default_memory_buffer() -> u64 {\n    134_217_728 // 128 MiB\n}\nfn default_chunk_size() -> u64 {\n    268_435_456 // 256 MiB\n}\n\nimpl Default for DumpImportConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            mode: default_mode(),\n            batch_size: default_batch_size(),\n            parallel_target_writes: default_parallel(),\n            memory_buffer_bytes: default_memory_buffer(),\n            chunk_size_bytes: default_chunk_size(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Dump import phase.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum DumpImportPhase {\n    /// No import in progress.\n    Idle = 0,\n    /// Reading and parsing dump.\n    Reading = 1,\n    /// Routing documents to target nodes.\n    Routing = 2,\n    /// Applying index settings.\n    ApplyingSettings = 3,\n    /// Completed successfully.\n    Complete = 4,\n    /// Failed with error.\n    Failed = 5,\n}\n\n/// Dump import status.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct DumpImportStatus {\n    /// Import ID.\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Target index UID.\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    /// Current phase.\n    pub phase: DumpImportPhase,\n    /// Documents processed so far.\n    pub documents_processed: u64,\n    /// Total documents (estimated).\n    pub total_documents: u64,\n    /// Bytes read so far.\n    pub bytes_read: u64,\n    /// Phase started at (UNIX ms).\n    pub phase_started_at: u64,\n    /// Error message if any.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Dump import manager.\npub struct DumpImportManager {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: DumpImportConfig,\n    /// Active imports (ID -> status).\n    active_imports: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, DumpImportStatus>>>,\n    /// Topology for routing.\n    topology: Arc<Topology>,\n}\n\nimpl DumpImportManager {\n    /// Create a new dump import manager.\n    pub fn new(config: DumpImportConfig, topology: Arc<Topology>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            active_imports: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            topology,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start a streaming dump import.\n    pub async fn start_import(\n        &self,\n        index_uid: String,\n        dump_data: Vec<u8>,\n        primary_key: String,\n        shard_count: u32,\n    ) -> Result<String> {\n        if self.config.mode != \"streaming\" {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\n                \"streaming dump import is disabled\".into(),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let import_id = format!(\"dump-{}-{}\", index_uid, uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n        let now = millis_now();\n\n        // Create initial status\n        let status = DumpImportStatus {\n            id: import_id.clone(),\n            index_uid: index_uid.clone(),\n            phase: DumpImportPhase::Reading,\n            documents_processed: 0,\n            total_documents: 0,\n            bytes_read: 0,\n            phase_started_at: now,\n            error: None,\n        };\n\n        {\n            let mut imports = self.active_imports.write().await;\n            imports.insert(import_id.clone(), status);\n        }\n\n        // Clone import_id before moving into the async block\n        let import_id_for_spawn = import_id.clone();\n\n        // Spawn background import task\n        let imports = self.active_imports.clone();\n        let topology = self.topology.clone();\n        let config = self.config.clone();\n\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            if let Err(e) = Self::run_import(\n                &import_id_for_spawn,\n                index_uid,\n                dump_data,\n                primary_key,\n                shard_count,\n                topology,\n                config,\n                imports,\n            )\n            .await\n            {\n                tracing::error!(\"Dump import {} failed: {}\", import_id_for_spawn, e);\n            }\n        });\n\n        Ok(import_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the status of an import.\n    pub async fn get_status(&self, import_id: &str) -> Option<DumpImportStatus> {\n        let imports = self.active_imports.read().await;\n        imports.get(import_id).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Run the import pipeline.\n    async fn run_import(\n        import_id: &str,\n        index_uid: String,\n        dump_data: Vec<u8>,\n        primary_key: String,\n        shard_count: u32,\n        topology: Arc<Topology>,\n        config: DumpImportConfig,\n        imports: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, DumpImportStatus>>>,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        // Update phase to reading\n        Self::update_phase(&imports, import_id, DumpImportPhase::Reading).await;\n\n        // Parse NDJSON and route documents\n        let data_str = std::str::from_utf8(&dump_data)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"invalid UTF-8 in dump: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        // Per-target buffers\n        let mut per_target_buffers: HashMap<(NodeId, u32), Vec<serde_json::Value>> =\n            HashMap::new();\n\n        let mut processed = 0u64;\n        let _total_estimate = 0u64;\n\n        for line in data_str.lines() {\n            if line.is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n            let doc: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(line).map_err(|e| {\n                MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"invalid JSON in dump: {}\", e))\n            })?;\n\n            // Extract primary key value\n            let pk_value = doc\n                .get(&primary_key)\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .ok_or_else(|| {\n                    MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n                        \"missing or invalid primary key field: {}\",\n                        primary_key\n                    ))\n                })?;\n\n            // Compute shard and route\n            let shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count);\n\n            // Get target nodes for this shard (assign across all replica groups)\n            let target_nodes: Vec<NodeId> = topology\n                .groups()\n                .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n                .collect();\n\n            if target_nodes.is_empty() {\n                return Err(MiroirError::Topology(format!(\"no nodes for shard {}\", shard_id)));\n            }\n\n            // Add to each target's buffer\n            for node in &target_nodes {\n                per_target_buffers\n                    .entry((node.clone(), shard_id))\n                    .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n                    .push(doc.clone());\n            }\n\n            processed += 1;\n\n            // Flush buffers when they reach batch size\n            if processed % config.batch_size as u64 == 0 {\n                Self::flush_buffers(\n                    &index_uid,\n                    &mut per_target_buffers,\n                    &config,\n                    &imports,\n                    import_id,\n                    processed,\n                )\n                .await?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Final flush\n        Self::flush_buffers(\n            &index_uid,\n            &mut per_target_buffers,\n            &config,\n            &imports,\n            import_id,\n            processed,\n        )\n        .await?;\n\n        // Mark complete\n        Self::update_phase(&imports, import_id, DumpImportPhase::Complete).await;\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Flush buffered documents to target nodes.\n    async fn flush_buffers(\n        index_uid: &str,\n        buffers: &mut HashMap<(NodeId, u32), Vec<serde_json::Value>>,\n        _config: &DumpImportConfig,\n        imports: &Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, DumpImportStatus>>>,\n        import_id: &str,\n        processed: u64,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        for ((node, _shard), docs) in buffers.drain() {\n            if docs.is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // POST documents to the node\n            // In a real implementation, this would use the HTTP client\n            tracing::debug!(\n                \"Flushing {} documents to node {} for index {}\",\n                docs.len(),\n                node,\n                index_uid\n            );\n\n            // Update status\n            let mut imports = imports.write().await;\n            if let Some(status) = imports.get_mut(import_id) {\n                status.documents_processed = processed;\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Update the phase of an import.\n    async fn update_phase(\n        imports: &Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, DumpImportStatus>>>,\n        import_id: &str,\n        phase: DumpImportPhase,\n    ) {\n        let mut imports = imports.write().await;\n        if let Some(status) = imports.get_mut(import_id) {\n            status.phase = phase;\n            status.phase_started_at = millis_now();\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as u64\n}\n\nimpl 1000);\n        assert_eq!(config.parallel_target_writes, 8);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_phase_serialization() {\n        let phase = DumpImportPhase::Routing;\n        let json = Dump import chunking for Mode C coordinator (plan §13.9 + §14.5).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Splits large NDJSON dumps into chunks on line boundaries.\n4\t//! Each chunk can be processed independently by any pod.\n5\t\n6\tuse crate::mode_c_coordinator::JobChunk;\n7\tuse std::io::{BufRead, BufReader, Cursor};\n8\t\n9\t/// Chunk specification for a dump import.\n10\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n11\tpub struct DumpChunkSpec {\n12\t    /// Chunk index (0-based).\n13\t    pub index: u32,\n14\t    /// Total number of chunks.\n15\t    pub total: u32,\n16\t    /// Starting byte offset.\n17\t    pub start_offset: u64,\n18\t    /// Ending byte offset.\n19\t    pub end_offset: u64,\n20\t    /// Estimated size in bytes.\n21\t    pub size_bytes: u64,\n22\t}\n23\t\n24\t/// Split dump data into chunks on line boundaries.\n25\t///\n26\t/// Returns a vector of chunk specifications. Each chunk contains the\n27\t/// byte offsets for processing that chunk of the dump.\n28\t///\n29\t/// # Arguments\n30\t/// * `data` - The full dump data (NDJSON)\n31\t/// * `chunk_size_bytes` - Target chunk size in bytes\n32\t///\n33\t/// # Returns\n34\t/// A vector of chunk specifications\n35\tpub fn split_dump_into_chunks(data: &[u8], chunk_size_bytes: u64) -> Vec<DumpChunkSpec> {\n36\t    if data.is_empty() {\n37\t        return Vec::new();\n38\t    }\n39\t\n40\t    let total_size = data.len() as u64;\n41\t\n42\t    // If the data is smaller than the chunk size, return a single chunk\n43\t    if total_size <= chunk_size_bytes {\n44\t        return vec![DumpChunkSpec {\n45\t            index: 0,\n46\t            total: 1,\n47\t            start_offset: 0,\n48\t            end_offset: total_size,\n49\t            size_bytes: total_size,\n50\t        }];\n51\t    }\n52\t\n53\t    let mut chunks = Vec::new();\n54\t    let mut current_offset: u64 = 0;\n55\t    let mut chunk_index = 0u32;\n56\t\n57\t    // Use a cursor to read through the data\n58\t    let cursor = Cursor::new(data);\n59\t    let reader = BufReader::new(cursor);\n60\t\n61\t    // Track line boundaries for chunking\n62\t    let mut line_start = 0u64;\n63\t    let mut last_line_end = 0u64;\n64\t\n65\t    for line_result in reader.lines() {\n66\t        match line_result {\n67\t            Ok(line) => {\n68\t                let line_bytes = line.len() as u64 + 1; // +1 for newline\n69\t                let line_end = last_line_end + line_bytes;\n70\t\n71\t                // Check if we've exceeded the chunk size since the last chunk start\n72\t                if line_end - current_offset >= chunk_size_bytes && current_offset < last_line_end {\n73\t                    // Create a chunk up to the previous line end\n74\t                    chunks.push(DumpChunkSpec {\n75\t                        index: chunk_index,\n76\t                        total: 0, // Will be filled in later\n77\t                        start_offset: current_offset,\n78\t                        end_offset: last_line_end,\n79\t                        size_bytes: last_line_end - current_offset,\n80\t                    });\n81\t\n82\t                    chunk_index += 1;\n83\t                    current_offset = last_line_end;\n84\t                }\n85\t\n86\t                last_line_end = line_end;\n87\t            }\n88\t            Err(_) => break,\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    // Add the final chunk\n93\t    if current_offset < total_size {\n94\t        chunks.push(DumpChunkSpec {\n95\t            index: chunk_index,\n96\t            total: 0, // Will be filled in later\n97\t            start_offset: current_offset,\n98\t            end_offset: total_size,\n99\t            size_bytes: total_size - current_offset,\n100\t        });\n101\t    }\n102\t\n103\t    // Update the total count for all chunks\n104\t    let total = chunks.len() as u32;\n105\t    for chunk in &mut chunks {\n106\t        chunk.total = total;\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    chunks\n110\t}\n111\t\n112\t/// Convert dump chunk specs to job chunks for the Mode C coordinator.\n113\tpub fn dump_specs_to_job_chunks(specs: Vec<DumpChunkSpec>) -> Vec<JobChunk> {\n114\t    specs\n115\t        .into_iter()\n116\t        .map(|spec| JobChunk {\n117\t            index: spec.index,\n118\t            total: spec.total,\n119\t            start: spec.start_offset.to_string(),\n120\t            end: spec.end_offset.to_string(),\n121\t            size_bytes: spec.size_bytes,\n122\t        })\n123\t        .collect()\n124\t}\n125\t\n126\t/// Extract a chunk of data from the full dump.\n127\t///\n128\t/// Returns the byte slice for the specified chunk.\n129\tpub fn extract_chunk_data<'a>(data: &'a [u8], chunk: &DumpChunkSpec) -> &'a [u8] {\n130\t    let start = chunk.start_offset as usize;\n131\t    let end = chunk.end_offset as usize;\n132\t    &data[start..end]\n133\t}\n134\t\n135\t#[cfg(test)]\n136\tmod tests {\n137\t    use super::*;\n138\t\n139\t    fn create_test_data(lines: usize, line_size: usize) -> Vec<u8> {\n140\t        let mut data = Vec::new();\n141\t        for i in 0..lines {\n142\t            let line = format!(\"{{\\\"id\\\": {}, \\\"data\\\": \\\"{}\\\"}}\\n\", i, \"x\".repeat(line_size));\n143\t            data.extend_from_slice(line.as_bytes());\n144\t        }\n145\t        data\n146\t    }\n147\t\n148\t    #[test]\n149\t    fn test_empty_data() {\n150\t        let data = Vec::new();\n151\t        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, 1024);\n152\t        assert!(chunks.is_empty());\n153\t    }\n154\t\n155\t    #[test]\n156\t    fn test_small_data() {\n157\t        let data = b\"{\\\"id\\\": 1}\\n{\\\"id\\\": 2}\\n\".to_vec();\n158\t        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, 1024);\n159\t        assert_eq!(chunks.len(), 1);\n160\t        assert_eq!(chunks[0].start_offset, 0);\n161\t        assert_eq!(chunks[0].end_offset, data.len() as u64);\n162\t    }\n163\t\n164\t    #[test]\n165\t    fn test_single_chunk() {\n166\t        let data = create_test_data(10, 50);\n167\t        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, 10_000);\n168\t        assert_eq!(chunks.len(), 1);\n169\t        assert_eq!(chunks[0].index, 0);\n170\t        assert_eq!(chunks[0].total, 1);\n171\t    }\n172\t\n173\t    #[test]\n174\t    fn test_multiple_chunks() {\n175\t        // Create data that will split into multiple chunks\n176\t        // Each line is about 70 bytes, so 100 lines = ~7KB\n177\t        let data = create_test_data(100, 50);\n178\t        let chunk_size = 2_000; // Should get ~3-4 chunks\n179\t        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, chunk_size);\n180\t\n181\t        assert!(chunks.len() > 1);\n182\t\n183\t        // Verify chunks are sequential and cover the full range\n184\t        let mut last_end = 0;\n185\t        for (i, chunk) in chunks.iter().enumerate() {\n186\t            assert_eq!(chunk.index, i as u32);\n187\t            assert_eq!(chunk.start_offset, last_end);\n188\t            assert!(chunk.end_offset > chunk.start_offset);\n189\t            last_end = chunk.end_offset;\n190\t        }\n191\t\n192\t        // Last chunk should end at the data size\n193\t        assert_eq!(chunks.last().unwrap().end_offset, data.len() as u64);\n194\t    }\n195\t\n196\t    #[test]\n197\t    fn test_chunk_boundaries_on_lines() {\n198\t        let data = create_test_data(20, 50);\n199\t        let chunk_size = 500;\n200\t        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, chunk_size);\n201\t\n202\t        // Verify each chunk starts and ends on line boundaries\n203\t Dump import chunking for Mode C coordinator (plan §13.9 + §14.5).\n//!\n//! Splits large NDJSON dumps into chunks on line boundaries.\n//! Each chunk can be processed independently by any pod.\n\nuse crate::mode_c_coordinator::JobChunk;\nuse std::io::{BufRead, BufReader, Cursor};\n\n/// Chunk specification for a dump import.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct DumpChunkSpec {\n    /// Chunk index (0-based).\n    pub index: u32,\n    /// Total number of chunks.\n    pub total: u32,\n    /// Starting byte offset.\n    pub start_offset: u64,\n    /// Ending byte offset.\n    pub end_offset: u64,\n    /// Estimated size in bytes.\n    pub size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\n/// Split dump data into chunks on line boundaries.\n///\n/// Returns a vector of chunk specifications. Each chunk contains the\n/// byte offsets for processing that chunk of the dump.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `data` - The full dump data (NDJSON)\n/// * `chunk_size_bytes` - Target chunk size in bytes\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// A vector of chunk specifications\npub fn split_dump_into_chunks(data: &[u8], chunk_size_bytes: u64) -> Vec<DumpChunkSpec> {\n    if data.is_empty() {\n        return Vec::new();\n    }\n\n    let total_size = data.len() as u64;\n\n    // If the data is smaller than the chunk size, return a single chunk\n    if total_size <= chunk_size_bytes {\n        return vec![DumpChunkSpec {\n            index: 0,\n            total: 1,\n            start_offset: 0,\n            end_offset: total_size,\n            size_bytes: total_size,\n        }];\n    }\n\n    let mut chunks = Vec::new();\n    let mut current_offset: u64 = 0;\n    let mut chunk_index = 0u32;\n\n    // Use a cursor to read through the data\n    let cursor = Cursor::new(data);\n    let reader = BufReader::new(cursor);\n\n    // Track line boundaries for chunking\n    let mut line_start = 0u64;\n    let mut last_line_end = 0u64;\n\n    for line_result in reader.lines() {\n        match line_result {\n            Ok(line) => {\n                let line_bytes = line.len() as u64 + 1; // +1 for newline\n                let line_end = last_line_end + line_bytes;\n\n                // Check if we've exceeded the chunk size since the last chunk start\n                if line_end - current_offset >= chunk_size_bytes && current_offset < last_line_end {\n                    // Create a chunk up to the previous line end\n                    chunks.push(DumpChunkSpec {\n                        index: chunk_index,\n                        total: 0, // Will be filled in later\n                        start_offset: current_offset,\n                        end_offset: last_line_end,\n                        size_bytes: last_line_end - current_offset,\n                    });\n\n                    chunk_index += 1;\n                    current_offset = last_line_end;\n                }\n\n                last_line_end = line_end;\n            }\n            Err(_) => break,\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Add the final chunk\n    if current_offset < total_size {\n        chunks.push(DumpChunkSpec {\n            index: chunk_index,\n            total: 0, // Will be filled in later\n            start_offset: current_offset,\n            end_offset: total_size,\n            size_bytes: total_size - current_offset,\n        });\n    }\n\n    // Update the total count for all chunks\n    let total = chunks.len() as u32;\n    for chunk in &mut chunks {\n        chunk.total = total;\n    }\n\n    chunks\n}\n\n/// Convert dump chunk specs to job chunks for the Mode C coordinator.\npub fn dump_specs_to_job_chunks(specs: Vec<DumpChunkSpec>) -> Vec<JobChunk> {\n    specs\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|spec| JobChunk {\n            index: spec.index,\n            total: spec.total,\n            start: spec.start_offset.to_string(),\n            end: spec.end_offset.to_string(),\n            size_bytes: spec.size_bytes,\n        })\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Extract a chunk of data from the full dump.\n///\n/// Returns the byte slice for the specified chunk.\npub fn extract_chunk_data<'a>(data: &'a [u8], chunk: &DumpChunkSpec) -> &'a [u8] {\n    let start = chunk.start_offset as usize;\n    let end = chunk.end_offset as usize;\n    &data[start..end]\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    fn create_test_data(lines: usize, line_size: usize) -> Vec<u8> {\n        let mut data = Vec::new();\n        for i in 0..lines {\n            let line = format!(\"{{\\\"id\\\": {}, \\\"data\\\": \\\"{}\\\"}}\\n\", i, \"x\".repeat(line_size));\n            data.extend_from_slice(line.as_bytes());\n        }\n        data\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_empty_data() {\n        let data = Vec::new();\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, 1024);\n        assert!(chunks.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_small_data() {\n        let data = b\"{\\\"id\\\": 1}\\n{\\\"id\\\": 2}\\n\".to_vec();\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, 1024);\n        assert_eq!(chunks.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].start_offset, 0);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].end_offset, data.len() as u64);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_single_chunk() {\n        let data = create_test_data(10, 50);\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, 10_000);\n        assert_eq!(chunks.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].index, 0);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].total, 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_multiple_chunks() {\n        // Create data that will split into multiple chunks\n        // Each line is about 70 bytes, so 100 lines = ~7KB\n        let data = create_test_data(100, 50);\n        let chunk_size = 2_000; // Should get ~3-4 chunks\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, chunk_size);\n\n        assert!(chunks.len() > 1);\n\n        // Verify chunks are sequential and cover the full range\n        let mut last_end = 0;\n        for (i, chunk) in chunks.iter().enumerate() {\n            assert_eq!(chunk.index, i as u32);\n            assert_eq!(chunk.start_offset, last_end);\n            assert!(chunk.end_offset > chunk.start_offset);\n            last_end = chunk.end_offset;\n        }\n\n        // Last chunk should end at the data size\n        assert_eq!(chunks.last().unwrap().end_offset, data.len() as u64);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_chunk_boundaries_on_lines() {\n        let data = create_test_data(20, 50);\n        let chunk_size = 500;\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, chunk_size);\n\n        // Verify each chunk starts and ends on line boundaries\n        for chunk in &chunks {\n            let chunk_data = extract_chunk_data(&data, chunk);\n\n            // Should start with valid JSON\n            assert!(chunk_data.starts_with(b\"{\"));\n            assert!(chunk_data.ends_with(b\"\\n\") || chunk_data.ends_with(b\"}\"));\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_chunk() {\n        let data = b\"line1\\nline2\\nline3\\n\".to_vec();\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, 5);\n\n        for chunk in &chunks {\n            let chunk_data = extract_chunk_data(&data, chunk);\n            // Verify the chunk data is within bounds\n            assert!(chunk_data.len() <= (chunk.end_offset - chunk.start_offset) as usize);\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_specs_to_job_chunks() {\n        let specs = vec![\n            DumpChunkSpec {\n                index: 0,\n                total: 2,\n                start_offset: 0,\n                end_offset: 100,\n                size_bytes: 100,\n            },\n            DumpChunkSpec {\n                index: 1,\n                total: 2,\n                start_offset: 100,\n                end_offset: 200,\n                size_bytes: 100,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        let job_chunks = dump_specs_to_job_chunks(specs);\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[0].index, 0);\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[0].total, 2);\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[0].start, \"0\");\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[0].end, \"100\");\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[1].index, 1);\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[1].start, \"100\");\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[1].end, \"200\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_large_file_chunking() {\n        // Simulate a 1GB file split into 256MB chunks\n        let line_size = 100;\n        let lines_per_chunk = (256 * 1024 * 1024) / line_size;\n        let total_lines = lines_per_chunk * 4; // 4 chunks\n\n        let data = create_test_data(total_lines as usize, line_size - 20);\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, 256 * 1024 * 1024);\n\n        // Should get approximately 4 chunks\n        assert!(chunks.len() >= 3 && chunks.len() <= 5);\n\n        // Verify total coverage\n        let total_covered: u64 = chunks.iter().map(|c| c.size_bytes).sum();\n        assert_eq!(total_covered, data.len() as u64);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_chunks_cover_full_data() {\n        let data = create_test_data(1000, 100);\n        let chunks = split_dump_into_chunks(&data, 50_000);\n\n        let mut total_size = 0u64;\n        for chunk in &chunks {\n            total_size += chunk.size_bytes;\n        }\n\n        assert_eq!(total_size, data.len() as u64);\n    }\n}\n","numLines":285,"startLine":1,"totalLines":285}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_3016f1c249bd4167a99cbd92","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Mode C worker loop for processing chunked background jobs (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! The worker continuously polls for queued jobs, claims them, processes them,\n4\t//! and renews claims. Large jobs are split into chunks; chunk jobs execute\n5\t//! the actual work (dump import, reshard backfill).\n6\t\n7\t#[cfg(test)]\n8\tmod acceptance_tests;\n9\t\n10\tuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\n11\tuse crate::mode_c_coordinator::{ClaimedJob, JobChunk, JobParams, JobProgress, JobType, ModeCCoordinator};\n12\tuse crate::reshard_chunking;\n13\tuse crate::task_store::TaskStore;\n14\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n15\tuse std::time::Duration;\n16\tuse tokio::time::interval;\n17\tuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\n18\t\n19\t/// Mode C worker configuration.\n20\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n21\tpub struct ModeCWorkerConfig {\n22\t    /// Poll interval for claiming new jobs.\n23\t    pub poll_interval_ms: u64,\n24\t    /// Heartbeat interval for renewing claims.\n25\t    pub heartbeat_interval_ms: u64,\n26\t    /// Maximum concurrent jobs per worker.\n27\t    pub max_concurrent_jobs: usize,\n28\t}\n29\t\n30\timpl Default for ModeCWorkerConfig {\n31\t    fn default() -> Self {\n32\t        Self {\n33\t            poll_interval_ms: 1000,      // 1 second\n34\t            heartbeat_interval_ms: 10000, // 10 seconds\n35\t            max_concurrent_jobs: 3,\n36\t        }\n37\t    }\n38\t}\n39\t\n40\t/// Mode C worker for processing background jobs.\n41\tpub struct ModeCWorker {\n42\t    /// Mode C coordinator.\n43\t    coordinator: ModeCCoordinator,\n44\t    /// Worker configuration.\n45\t    config: ModeCWorkerConfig,\n46\t    /// Currently running jobs.\n47\t    running_jobs: Arc<tokio::sync::RwLock<Vec<RunningJob>>>,\n48\t}\n49\t\n50\t/// A job currently being processed by this worker.\n51\tstruct RunningJob {\n52\t    /// Job ID.\n53\t    id: String,\n54\t    /// Job type.\n55\t    type_: JobType,\n56\t    /// Job parameters.\n57\t    params: JobParams,\n58\t    /// Last heartbeat time.\n59\t    last_heartbeat: i64,\n60\t}\n61\t\n62\timpl ModeCWorker {\n63\t    /// Create a new Mode C worker.\n64\t    pub fn new(\n65\t        task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n66\t        pod_id: String,\n67\t        config: ModeCWorkerConfig,\n68\t    ) -> Self {\n69\t        let coordinator = ModeCCoordinator::new(task_store, pod_id)\n70\t            .with_claim_ttl_ms(30_000) // 30 seconds\n71\t            .with_heartbeat_interval_ms(config.heartbeat_interval_ms as i64);\n72\t\n73\t        Self {\n74\t            coordinator,\n75\t            config,\n76\t            running_jobs: Arc::new(tokio::sync::RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n77\t        }\n78\t    }\n79\t\n80\t    /// Start the worker loop.\n81\t    ///\n82\t    /// This runs continuously, polling for jobs and processing them.\n83\t    pub async fn run(&self) -> Result<()> {\n84\t        info!(\"Starting Mode C worker loop\");\n85\t\n86\t        let mut poll_interval = interval(Duration::from_millis(self.config.poll_interval_ms));\n87\t        let mut heartbeat_interval = interval(Duration::from_millis(self.config.heartbeat_interval_ms));\n88\t\n89\t        loop {\n90\t            tokio::select! {\n91\t                _ = poll_interval.tick() => {\n92\t                    if let Err(e) = self.poll_and_claim().await {\n93\t                        error!(\"Error polling for jobs: {}\", e);\n94\t                    }\n95\t                }\n96\t                _ = heartbeat_interval.tick() => {\n97\t                    if let Err(e) = self.renew_claims().await {\n98\t                        error!(\"Error renewing claims: {}\", e);\n99\t                    }\n100\t                }\n101\t            }\n102\t        }\n103\t    }\n104\t\n105\t    /// Poll for queued jobs and claim one if available.\n106\t    async fn poll_and_claim(&self) -> Result<()> {\n107\t        // Check if we're at capacity\n108\t        let running = self.running_jobs.read().await;\n109\t        if running.len() >= self.config.max_concurrent_jobs {\n110\t            debug!(\"At capacity ({} jobs), skipping poll\", running.len());\n111\t            return Ok(());\n112\t        }\n113\t        drop(running);\n114\t\n115\t        // Reclaim expired claims first\n116\t        let reclaimed = self.coordinator.reclaim_expired_claims()?;\n117\t        if reclaimed > 0 {\n118\t            info!(\"Reclaimed {} expired job claims\", reclaimed);\n119\t        }\n120\t\n121\t        // Try to claim a job\n122\t        let claimed = match self.coordinator.claim_job()? {\n123\t            Some(job) => job,\n124\t            None => return Ok(()), // No jobs available\n125\t        };\n126\t\n127\t        let job_id = claimed.id.clone();\n128\t        let job_type_str = claimed.type_.clone();\n129\t\n130\t        info!(\"Claimed job {} (type: {})\", job_id, job_type_str);\n131\t\n132\t        // Parse job type and parameters\n133\t        let job_type = JobType::from_str(&claimed.type_)\n134\t            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"unknown job type: {}\", claimed.type_)))?;\n135\t        let params = claimed.parse_params()?;\n136\t\n137\t        // Check if this is a large job that needs chunking\n138\t        if claimed.parent_job_id.is_none() && self.should_chunk(&job_type, &params) {\n139\t            // Split into chunks and re-enqueue\n140\t            self.split_and_enqueue(&claimed, &job_type, &params).await?;\n141\t            return Ok(());\n142\t        }\n143\t\n144\t        // Spawn a task to process the job\n145\t        let running_job = RunningJob {\n146\t            id: job_id.clone(),\n147\t            type_: job_type,\n148\t            params: params.clone(),\n149\t            last_heartbeat: crate::mode_c_coordinator::now_ms(),\n150\t        };\n151\t\n152\t        {\n153\t            let mut running = self.running_jobs.write().await;\n154\t            running.push(running_job);\n155\t        }\n156\t\n157\t        let coordinator = self.coordinator.clone();\n158\t        let running_jobs = self.running_jobs.clone();\n159\t        let job_id_clone = job_id.clone();\n160\t\n161\t        tokio::spawn(async move {\n162\t            let result = match job_type {\n163\t                JobType::DumpImport => Self::process_dump_import(&coordinator, &job_id_clone, &params).await,\n164\t                JobType::ReshardBackfill => Self::process_reshard_backfill(&coordinator, &job_id_clone, &params).await,\n165\t            };\n166\t\n167\t            // Remove from running jobs\n168\t            {\n169\t                let mut running = running_jobs.write().await;\n170\t                running.retain(|j| j.id != job_id_clone);\n171\t            }\n172\t\n173\t            if let Err(e) = result {\n174\t                error!(\"Job {} failed: {}\", job_id_clone, e);\n175\t                let progress = JobProgress::default();\n176\t                let _ = coordinator.fail_job(&job_id_clone, &progress, e.to_string());\n177\t            }\n178\t        });\n179\t\n180\t        Ok(())\n181\t    }\n182\t\n183\t    /// Renew claims for all running jobs.\n184\t    async fn renew_claims(&self) -> Result<()> {\n185\t        let running = self.running_jobs.read().await;\n186\t        let now = crate::mode_c_coordinator::now_ms();\n187\t\n188\t        for job in running.iter() {\n189\t            match self.coordinator.renew_claim(&job.id) {\n190\t                Ok(true) => {\n191\t                    debug!(\"Renewed claim for job {}\", job.id);\n192\t                }\n193\t                Ok(false) => {\n194\t                    warn!(\"Failed to renew claim for job {} - may have lost ownership\", job.id);\n195\t                }\n196\t                Err(e) => {\n197\t                    error!(\"Error renewing claim for job {}: {}\", job.id, e);\n198\t                }\n199\t            }\n200\t        }\n201\t\n202\t        Ok(())\n203\t    }\n204\t\n205\t    /// Check if a job should be split into chunks.\n206\t    fn should_chunk(&self, job_type: &JobType, params: &JobParams) -> bool {\n207\t        const DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE_BYTES: u64 = 268_435_456; // 256 MiB\n208\t\n209\t        match job_type {\n210\t            JobType::DumpImport => {\n211\t                // Chunk if source size exceeds 2x the default chunk size\n212\t                if let Some(size) = params.source_size_bytes {\n213\t                    size > DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE_BYTES * 2\n214\t                } else {\n215\t                    false\n216\t                }\n217\t            }\n218\t            JobType::ReshardBackfill => {\n219\t                // Chunk if old_shards exceeds 32 (configurable threshold)\n220\t                if let Some(old_shards) = params.old_shards {\n221\t                    old_shards > 32\n222\t                } else {\n223\t                    false\n224\t                }\n225\t            }\n226\t        }\n227\t    }\n228\t\n229\t    /// Split a large job into chunks and enqueue them.\n230\t    async fn split_and_enqueue(\n231\t        &self,\n232\t        claimed: &ClaimedJob,\n233\t        job_type: &JobType,\n234\t        params: &JobParams,\n235\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n236\t        let chunk_size_bytes = self.coordinator.default_chunk_size_bytes();\n237\t\n238\t        let chunks = match job_type {\n239\t            JobType::DumpImport => {\n240\t                // For dump import, split on byte offset boundaries\n241\t                // In a full implementation, we would:\n242\t                // 1. Fetch the dump data from params.source_url\n243\t                // 2. Use dump_chunking::split_dump_into_chunks to split on NDJSON line boundaries\n244\t                // For now, we create size-based chunks that will be aligned to line boundaries\n245\t                // during actual processing by the worker that processes each chunk\n246\t                let source_size = params.source_size_bytes.unwrap_or(0);\n247\t                if source_size == 0 {\n248\t                    return Err(MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"source_size_bytes is required for dump import chunking\".into()));\n249\t                }\n250\t\n251\t                // Calculate number of chunks (ceiling division)\n252\t                let total_chunks = ((source_size + chunk_size_bytes - 1) / chunk_size_bytes) as u32;\n253\t\n254\t                (0..total_chunks)\n255\t                    .map(|i| {\n256\t                        let i = i as u64;\n257\t                        let start = i * chunk_size_bytes;\n258\t                        let end = std::cmp::min(start + chunk_size_bytes, source_size);\n259\t                        JobChunk {\n260\t                            index: i as u32,\n261\t                            total: total_chunks,\n262\t                            start: start.to_string(),\n263\t                            end: end.to_string(),\n264\t                            size_bytes: end - start,\n265\t                        }\n266\t                    })\n267\t                    .collect()\n268\t            }\n269\t            JobType::ReshardBackfill => {\n270\t                // Use reshard_chunking to split by shard-id range\n271\t                let old_shards = params.old_shards.unwrap_or(1);\n272\t                let target_shards = params.target_shards.unwrap_or(old_shards * 2);\n273\t                let shards_per_chunk = 32; // Configurable shard count per chunk\n274\t\n275\t                let specs = reshard_chunking::split_reshard_into_chunks(\n276\t                    old_shards,\n277\t                    target_shards,\n278\t                    shards_per_chunk,\n279\t                );\n280\t                reshard_chunking::reshard_specs_to_job_chunks(specs)\n281\t            }\n282\t        };\n283\t\n284\t        info!(\"Splitting job {} into {} chunks\", claimed.id, chunks.len());\n285\t        self.coordinator.split_job_into_chunks(claimed, chunks)?;\n286\t\n287\t        Ok(())\n288\t    }\n289\t\n290\t    /// Process a dump import job.\n291\t    async fn process_dump_import(\n292\t        coordinator: &ModeCCoordinator,\n293\t        job_id: &str,\n294\t        params: &JobParams,\n295\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n296\t        info!(\"Processing dump import job {}\", job_id);\n297\t\n298\t        // If this is a chunk job, process the chunk\n299\t        if let Some(chunk) = &params.chunk {\n300\t            info!(\n301\t                \"Processing dump chunk {}/{} (offsets {}-{})\",\n302\t                chunk.index,\n303\t                chunk.total,\n304\t                chunk.start,\n305\t                chunk.end\n306\t            );\n307\t\n308\t            // Parse chunk boundaries\n309\t            let start_offset: u64 =     .map_err(|_| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"invalid chunk end offset\".into()))?;\n313\t\n314\t            // Process the dump import chunk\n315\t            let result = Self::process_dump_chunk(\n316\t                coordinator,\n317\t                job_id,\n318\t                params,\n319\t                start_offset,\n320\t                end_offset,\n321\t            ).await;\n322\t\n323\t            match result {\n324\t                Ok((bytes_processed, docs_routed, last_cursor)) => {\n325\t                    let progress = JobProgress {\n326\t                        bytes_processed,\n327\t                        docs_routed,\n328\t                        last_cursor,\n329\t                        error: None,\n330\t                    };\n331\t      chunk.index, chunk.total, bytes_processed, docs_routed\n335\t                    );\n336\t                }\n337\t                Err(e) => {\n338\t                    let progress = JobProgress        error: Some(e.to_string()),\n343\t                    };\n344\t                    coordinator.fail_job(job_id, &progress, e.to_string())?;\n345\t                    return Err(e);\n346\t                }\n347\t            }\n348\t        } else {\n349\t            // Parent job was already split, mark as delegated\n350\t            let progress = JobProgress {\n351\t coordinator.complete_job(job_id, &progress)?;\n358\t        }\n359\t\n360\t        info!(\"Completed dump import job {}\", job_id);\n361\t\n362\t        Ok(())\n363\t    }\n364\t\n365\t    /// Process a single dump chunk.\n366\t    ///\n367\t    /// This is a simulation of the full dump import processing. In production,\n368\t    /// this would:\n369\t    /// 1. Fetch dump data from params.source_url with Range header\n370\t    /// 2. Parse NDJSON lines (align to line boundaries)\n371\t    /// 3. Handle idempotent resume from last_cursor\n374\t    async fn process_dump_chunk(\n375\t        coordinator: &ModeCCoordinator,\n376\t        job_id: &str,\n377\t        params: &JobParams,\n378\t        start_offset: u64,\n379\t        end_offset: u64,\n380\t    ) -> Result<(u64, u64, String)> {\n381\t        use crate::router::shard_for_key;\n382\t        use std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n383\t\n384\t        let chunk_size = end_offset - start_offset;\n385\t        let shard_count = params.shard_count.unwrap_or(64);\n386\t        let primary_key = params.primary_key.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"id\");\n387\t\n388\t        // Simulate processing in batches to allow for progress updates\n389\t        let batch_size = 10_000_000; // 10 MB batches\n390\t        let mut bytes_processed = 0u64;\n391\t        let mut docs_routed = 0u64;\n392\t        let mut last_cursor = start_offset.to_string();\n393\t\n394\t        let start_time = Instant::now();\n395\t        let heartbeat_interval = Duration::from_secs(5);\n396\t\n397\t        while bytes_processed < chunk_size {\n398\t            let batch_end = std::cmp::min(bytes_processed + batch_size, chunk_size);\n399\t            let batch_bytes = batch_end - bytes_processed;\n400\t\n401\t            // Simulate processing time based on batch size\n402\t            // In production, tokio::time::sleep(simulate_delay).await;\n405\t\n406\t            // Simulate document routing\n407\t            // Estimate ~100KB per document (typical JSON document)\n408\t            let last_cursor: start_shard.to_string(),\n492\t                        error: Some(e.to_string()),\n493\t                    };\n494\t                    coordinator.fail_job(job_id, &progress,
