In production,\n518\t    /// this would:\n519\t    /// 1. Read documents from old shard range [start_shard, end_shard)\n520\t    /// 2. Re-route each document to new shard configuration\n521\t    /// 3. Update progress periodically (heartbeat)\n522\t    /// 4. Handle idempotent resume from last_cursor\n523\t    async fn process_reshard_chunk(\n524\t        coordinator: &ModeCCoordinator,\n525\t        job_id: &str,\n526\t        params: &JobParams,\n527\t        start_shard: u32,\n528\t        end_shard: u32,\n529\t    ) -> Result<(u64, String)> {\n530\t        use crate::router::{shard_for_key, assign_shard_in_group};\n531\t        use crate::topology::{Topology, Group, NodeId};\n532\t        use std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n533\t\n534\t        let old_shards = params.old_shards.unwrap_or(64);\n535\t        let target_shards = params.target_shards.unwrap_or(128);\n536\t        let shard_count = (end_shard - start_shard) as u64;\n537\t\n538\t        // Simulate processing in batches to allow for progress updates\n539\t        let batch_shards = 4; // Process 4 shards at a time\n540\t        let mut shards_processed = 0u32;\n541\t        let mut docs_backfilled = 0u64;\n542\t        let mut last_cursor = start_shard.to_string();\n543\t\n544\t        let start_time = Instant::now();\n545\t        let heartbeat_interval = Duration::from_secs(5);\n546\t\n547\t        // Create a simple topology for routing simulation\n548\t        let topology = Topology::new(2, 1, 3); // 2 groups, 1 RF, 3 nodes per group\n549\t\n550\t        while start_shard + shards_processed < end_shard {\n551\t            let batch_end = std::cmp::min(start_shard + shards_processed + batch_shards, end_shard);\n552\t            let batch_count = (batch_end - (start_shard + shards_processed)) as u64;\n553\t\n554\t            // Simulate document processing for each shard\n555\t            // Estimate ~1000 documents per shard\n556\t            for shard_id in (start_shard + shards_processed)..batch_end {\n557\t                let estimated_docs = 1000u64;\n558\t\n559\t                for i in 0..estimated_docs {\n560\t                    // Simulate document key\n561\t                    let doc_key = format!(\"doc-shard{}-{}\", shard_id, i);\n562\t\n563\t                    // Compute old shard assignment\n564\t                    let _old_shard = shard_for_key(&doc_key, old_shards);\n565\t\n566\t                    // Compute new shard assignment\n567\t                    let new_shard = shard_for_key(&doc_key, target_shards);\n568\t\n569\t                    // In production, we would:\n570\t                    // 1. Read the document from the old shard\n571\t                    // 2. Write it to the new shard assignment\n572\t                    // 3. Handle any conflicts or duplicates\n573\t\n574\t                    // Simulate routing to replica groups\n575\t                    for group in topology.groups() {\n576\t                        let _targets = assign_shard_in_group(new_shard, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n577\t                        // In production, we would write to these target nodes\n578\t                    }\n579\t                }\n580\t\n581\t                docs_backfilled += estimated_docs;\n582\t            }\n583\t\n584\t            shards_processed = batch_end - start_shard;\n585\t            last_cursor = (start_shard + shards_processed as u32).to_string();\n586\t\n587\t            // Update progress periodically\n588\t            let elapsed = start_time.elapsed();\n589\t            if elapsed >= heartbeat_interval {\n590\t                // Update progress and renew claim\n591\t                let progress = JobProgress {\n592\t                    bytes_processed: 0,\n593\t                    docs_routed: docs_backfilled,\n594\t                    last_cursor: last_cursor.clone(),\n595\t                    error: None,\n596\t                };\n597\t                coordinator.update_progress(job_id, &progress, crate::mode_c_coordinator::JobState::InProgress)?;\n598\t\n599\t                // Renew the claim\n600\t                let _ = coordinator.renew_claim(job_id);\n601\t\n602\t                // Reset the timer\n603\t                let _ = start_time.elapsed();\n604\t            }\n605\t\n606\t            // Simulate processing time\n607\t            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n608\t        }\n609\t\n610\t        Ok((docs_backfilled, last_cursor))\n611\t    }\n612\t}\n613\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c3b258f2-678e-4ee3-83ca-eaf6717d389f","uuid":"518f9b87-66c3-4c66-9ff2-3228fcca467b","timestamp":"2026-05-23T11:08:14.611Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/mode_c_worker/mod.rs","content":"//! Mode C worker loop for processing chunked background jobs (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n//!\n//! The worker continuously polls for queued jobs, claims them, processes them,\n//! and renews claims. Large jobs are split into chunks; chunk jobs execute\n//! the actual work (dump import, reshard backfill).\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod acceptance_tests;\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::mode_c_coordinator::{ClaimedJob, JobChunk, JobParams, JobProgress, JobType, ModeCCoordinator};\nuse crate::reshard_chunking;\nuse crate::task_store::TaskStore;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::time::interval;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\n\n/// Mode C worker configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ModeCWorkerConfig {\n    /// Poll interval for claiming new jobs.\n    pub poll_interval_ms: u64,\n    /// Heartbeat interval for renewing claims.\n    pub heartbeat_interval_ms: u64,\n    /// Maximum concurrent jobs per worker.\n    pub max_concurrent_jobs: usize,\n}\n\nimpl Default for ModeCWorkerConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            poll_interval_ms: 1000,      // 1 second\n            heartbeat_interval_ms: 10000, // 10 seconds\n            max_concurrent_jobs: 3,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Mode C worker for processing background jobs.\npub struct ModeCWorker {\n    /// Mode C coordinator.\n    coordinator: ModeCCoordinator,\n    /// Worker configuration.\n    config: ModeCWorkerConfig,\n    /// Currently running jobs.\n    running_jobs: Arc<tokio::sync::RwLock<Vec<RunningJob>>>,\n}\n\n/// A job currently being processed by this worker.\nstruct RunningJob {\n    /// Job ID.\n    id: String,\n    /// Job type.\n    type_: JobType,\n    /// Job parameters.\n    params: JobParams,\n    /// Last heartbeat time.\n    last_heartbeat: i64,\n}\n\nimpl ModeCWorker {\n    /// Create a new Mode C worker.\n    pub fn new(\n        task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        pod_id: String,\n        config: ModeCWorkerConfig,\n    ) -> Self {\n        let coordinator = ModeCCoordinator::new(task_store, pod_id)\n            .with_claim_ttl_ms(30_000) // 30 seconds\n            .with_heartbeat_interval_ms(config.heartbeat_interval_ms as i64);\n\n        Self {\n            coordinator,\n            config,\n            running_jobs: Arc::new(tokio::sync::RwLock::new(Vec::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Start the worker loop.\n    ///\n    /// This runs continuously, polling for jobs and processing them.\n    pub async fn run(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        info!(\"Starting Mode C worker loop\");\n\n        let mut poll_interval = interval(Duration::from_millis(self.config.poll_interval_ms));\n        let mut heartbeat_interval = interval(Duration::from_millis(self.config.heartbeat_interval_ms));\n\n        loop {\n            tokio::select! {\n                _ = poll_interval.tick() => {\n                    if let Err(e) = self.poll_and_claim().await {\n                        error!(\"Error polling for jobs: {}\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n                _ = heartbeat_interval.tick() => {\n                    if let Err(e) = self.renew_claims().await {\n                        error!(\"Error renewing claims: {}\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Poll for queued jobs and claim one if available.\n    async fn poll_and_claim(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        // Check if we're at capacity\n        let running = self.running_jobs.read().await;\n        if running.len() >= self.config.max_concurrent_jobs {\n            debug!(\"At capacity ({} jobs), skipping poll\", running.len());\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n        drop(running);\n\n        // Reclaim expired claims first\n        let reclaimed = self.coordinator.reclaim_expired_claims()?;\n        if reclaimed > 0 {\n            info!(\"Reclaimed {} expired job claims\", reclaimed);\n        }\n\n        // Try to claim a job\n        let claimed = match self.coordinator.claim_job()? {\n            Some(job) => job,\n            None => return Ok(()), // No jobs available\n        };\n\n        let job_id = claimed.id.clone();\n        let job_type_str = claimed.type_.clone();\n\n        info!(\"Claimed job {} (type: {})\", job_id, job_type_str);\n\n        // Parse job type and parameters\n        let job_type = JobType::from_str(&claimed.type_)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"unknown job type: {}\", claimed.type_)))?;\n        let params = claimed.parse_params()?;\n\n        // Check if this is a large job that needs chunking\n        if claimed.parent_job_id.is_none() && self.should_chunk(&job_type, &params) {\n            // Split into chunks and re-enqueue\n            self.split_and_enqueue(&claimed, &job_type, &params).await?;\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        // Spawn a task to process the job\n        let running_job = RunningJob {\n            id: job_id.clone(),\n            type_: job_type,\n            params: params.clone(),\n            last_heartbeat: crate::mode_c_coordinator::now_ms(),\n        };\n\n        {\n            let mut running = self.running_jobs.write().await;\n            running.push(running_job);\n        }\n\n        let coordinator = self.coordinator.clone();\n        let running_jobs = self.running_jobs.clone();\n        let job_id_clone = job_id.clone();\n\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            let result = match job_type {\n                JobType::DumpImport => Self::process_dump_import(&coordinator, &job_id_clone, &params).await,\n                JobType::ReshardBackfill => Self::process_reshard_backfill(&coordinator, &job_id_clone, &params).await,\n            };\n\n            // Remove from running jobs\n            {\n                let mut running = running_jobs.write().await;\n                running.retain(|j| j.id != job_id_clone);\n            }\n\n            if let Err(e) = result {\n                error!(\"Job {} failed: {}\", job_id_clone, e);\n                let progress = JobProgress::default();\n                let _ = coordinator.fail_job(&job_id_clone, &progress, e.to_string());\n            }\n        });\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Renew claims for all running jobs.\n    async fn renew_claims(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let running = self.running_jobs.read().await;\n        let now = crate::mode_c_coordinator::now_ms();\n\n        for job in running.iter() {\n            match self.coordinator.renew_claim(&job.id) {\n                Ok(true) => {\n                    debug!(\"Renewed claim for job {}\", job.id);\n                }\n                Ok(false) => {\n                    warn!(\"Failed to renew claim for job {} - may have lost ownership\", job.id);\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    error!(\"Error renewing claim for job {}: {}\", job.id, e);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a job should be split into chunks.\n    fn should_chunk(&self, job_type: &JobType, params: &JobParams) -> bool {\n        const DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE_BYTES: u64 = 268_435_456; // 256 MiB\n\n        match job_type {\n            JobType::DumpImport => {\n                // Chunk if source size exceeds 2x the default chunk size\n                if let Some(size) = params.source_size_bytes {\n                    size > DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE_BYTES * 2\n                } else {\n                    false\n                }\n            }\n            JobType::ReshardBackfill => {\n                // Chunk if old_shards exceeds 32 (configurable threshold)\n                if let Some(old_shards) = params.old_shards {\n                    old_shards > 32\n                } else {\n                    false\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Split a large job into chunks and enqueue them.\n    async fn split_and_enqueue(\n        &self,\n        claimed: &ClaimedJob,\n        job_type: &JobType,\n        params: &JobParams,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let chunk_size_bytes = self.coordinator.default_chunk_size_bytes();\n\n        let chunks = match job_type {\n            JobType::DumpImport => {\n                // For dump import, split on byte offset boundaries\n                // In a full implementation, we would:\n                // 1. Fetch the dump data from params.source_url\n                // 2. Use dump_chunking::split_dump_into_chunks to split on NDJSON line boundaries\n                // For now, we create size-based chunks that will be aligned to line boundaries\n                // during actual processing by the worker that processes each chunk\n                let source_size = params.source_size_bytes.unwrap_or(0);\n                if source_size == 0 {\n                    return Err(MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"source_size_bytes is required for dump import chunking\".into()));\n                }\n\n                // Calculate number of chunks (ceiling division)\n                let total_chunks = ((source_size + chunk_size_bytes - 1) / chunk_size_bytes) as u32;\n\n                (0..total_chunks)\n                    .map(|i| {\n                        let i = i as u64;\n                        let start = i * chunk_size_bytes;\n                        let end = std::cmp::min(start + chunk_size_bytes, source_size);\n                        JobChunk {\n                            index: i as u32,\n                            total: total_chunks,\n                            start: start.to_string(),\n                            end: end.to_string(),\n                            size_bytes: end - start,\n                        }\n                    })\n                    .collect()\n            }\n            JobType::ReshardBackfill => {\n                // Use reshard_chunking to split by shard-id range\n                let old_shards = params.old_shards.unwrap_or(1);\n                let target_shards = params.target_shards.unwrap_or(old_shards * 2);\n                let shards_per_chunk = 32; // Configurable shard count per chunk\n\n                let specs = reshard_chunking::split_reshard_into_chunks(\n                    old_shards,\n                    target_shards,\n                    shards_per_chunk,\n                );\n                reshard_chunking::reshard_specs_to_job_chunks(specs)\n            }\n        };\n\n        info!(\"Splitting job {} into {} chunks\", claimed.id, chunks.len());\n        self.coordinator.split_job_into_chunks(claimed, chunks)?;\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Process a dump import job.\n    async fn process_dump_import(\n        coordinator: &ModeCCoordinator,\n        job_id: &str,\n        params: &JobParams,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        info!(\"Processing dump import job {}\", job_id);\n\n        // If this is a chunk job, process the chunk\n        if let Some(chunk) = &params.chunk {\n            info!(\n                \"Processing dump chunk {}/{} (offsets {}-{})\",\n                chunk.index,\n                chunk.total,\n                chunk.start,\n                chunk.end\n            );\n\n            // Parse chunk boundaries\n            let start_offset: u64 = chunk.start.parse()\n                .map_err(|_| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"invalid chunk start offset\".into()))?;\n            let end_offset: u64 = chunk.end.parse()\n                .map_err(|_| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\"invalid chunk end offset\".into()))?;\n\n            // Process the dump import chunk\n            let result = Self::process_dump_chunk(\n                coordinator,\n                job_id,\n                params,\n                start_offset,\n                end_offset,\n            ).await;\n\n            match result {\n                Ok((bytes_processed, docs_routed, last_cursor)) => {\n                    let progress = JobProgress {\n                        bytes_processed,\n                        docs_routed,\n                        last_cursor,\n                        error: None,\n                    };\n                    coordinator.complete_job(job_id, &progress)?;\n                    info!(\n                        \"Completed dump chunk {}/{}: {} bytes, {} docs\",\n                        chunk.index, chunk.total, bytes_processed, docs_routed\n                    );\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    let progress = JobProgress {\n                        bytes_processed: start_offset,\n                        docs_routed: 0,\n                        last_cursor: start_offset.to_string(),\n                        error: Some(e.to_string()),\n                    };\n                    coordinator.fail_job(job_id, &progress, e.to_string())?;\n                    return Err(e);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // Parent job was already split, mark as delegated\n            let progress = JobProgress {\n                bytes_processed: 0,\n                docs_routed: 0,\n                last_cursor: \"delegated\".to_string(),\n                error: None,\n            };\n\n            coordinator.complete_job(job_id, &progress)?;\n        }\n\n        info!(\"Completed dump import job {}\", job_id);\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Process a single dump chunk.\n    ///\n    /// This is a simulation of the full dump import processing. In production,\n    /// this would:\n    /// 1. Fetch dump data from params.source_url with Range header\n    /// 2. Parse NDJSON lines (align to line boundaries)\n    /// 3. Route each document to target shard based on primary_key\n    /// 4. Update progress periodically (heartbeat)\n    /// 5. Handle idempotent resume from last_cursor\n    async fn process_dump_chunk(\n        coordinator: &ModeCCoordinator,\n        job_id: &str,\n        params: &JobParams,\n        start_offset: u64,\n        end_offset: u64,\n    ) -> Result<(u64, u64, String)> {\n        use crate::router::shard_for_key;\n        use std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n\n        let chunk_size = end_offset - start_offset;\n        let shard_count = params.shard_count.unwrap_or(64);\n        let primary_key = params.primary_key.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"id\");\n\n        // Simulate processing in batches to allow for progress updates\n        let batch_size = 10_000_000; // 10 MB batches\n        let mut bytes_processed = 0u64;\n        let mut docs_routed = 0u64;\n        let mut last_cursor = start_offset.to_string();\n\n        let start_time = Instant::now();\n        let heartbeat_interval = Duration::from_secs(5);\n\n        while bytes_processed < chunk_size {\n            let batch_end = std::cmp::min(bytes_processed + batch_size, chunk_size);\n            let batch_bytes = batch_end - bytes_processed;\n\n            // Simulate processing time based on batch size\n            // In production, this would be actual I/O and processing\n            let simulate_delay = Duration::from_millis((batch_bytes / 1_000_000) * 10);\n            tokio::time::sleep(simulate_delay).await;\n\n            // Simulate document routing\n            // Estimate ~100KB per document (typical JSON document)\n            let estimated_docs = batch_bytes / 100_000;\n            for i in 0..estimated_docs {\n                // Simulate document key\n                let doc_key = format!(\"{}-{}-{}\", primary_key, start_offset + bytes_processed, i);\n                let _shard_id = shard_for_key(&doc_key, shard_count);\n                // In production, we would route the document to the target node/shard\n            }\n\n            bytes_processed = batch_end;\n            docs_routed += estimated_docs;\n            last_cursor = (start_offset + bytes_processed).to_string();\n\n            // Update progress periodically\n            let elapsed = start_time.elapsed();\n            if elapsed >= heartbeat_interval {\n                // Update progress and renew claim\n                let progress = JobProgress {\n                    bytes_processed,\n                    docs_routed,\n                    last_cursor: last_cursor.clone(),\n                    error: None,\n                };\n                coordinator.update_progress(job_id, &progress, crate::mode_c_coordinator::JobState::InProgress)?;\n\n                // Renew the claim\n                let _ = coordinator.renew_claim(job_id);\n\n                // Reset the timer\n                let _ = start_time.elapsed();\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok((bytes_processed, docs_routed, last_cursor))\n    }\n\n    /// Process a reshard backfill job.\n    async fn process_reshard_backfill(\n        coordinator: &ModeCCoordinator,\n        job_id: &str,\n        params: &JobParams,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        info!(\"Processing reshard backfill job {}\", job_id);\n\n        // If this is a chunk job, process the shard range\n        if let Some(chunk) = &params.chunk {\n            let (start_shard, end_shard) = reshard_chunking::parse_reshard_chunk(chunk)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"invalid chunk spec: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            info!(\n                \"Processing reshard chunk {}/{} (shards {}-{})\",\n                chunk.index,\n                chunk.total,\n                start_shard,\n                end_shard\n            );\n\n            // Process the reshard backfill chunk\n            let result = Self::process_reshard_chunk(\n                coordinator,\n                job_id,\n                params,\n                start_shard,\n                end_shard,\n            ).await;\n\n            match result {\n                Ok((docs_backfilled, last_cursor)) => {\n                    let progress = JobProgress {\n                        bytes_processed: 0, // Not applicable for reshard\n                        docs_routed: docs_backfilled,\n                        last_cursor,\n                        error: None,\n                    };\n                    coordinator.complete_job(job_id, &progress)?;\n                    info!(\n                        \"Completed reshard chunk {}/{}: {} docs from shards {}-{}\",\n                        chunk.index, chunk.total, docs_backfilled, start_shard, end_shard\n                    );\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    let progress = JobProgress {\n                        bytes_processed: 0,\n                        docs_routed: 0,\n                        last_cursor: start_shard.to_string(),\n                        error: Some(e.to_string()),\n                    };\n                    coordinator.fail_job(job_id, &progress, e.to_string())?;\n                    return Err(e);\n                }\n            }\n        } else {\n            // Parent job was already split, mark as delegated\n            let progress = JobProgress {\n                bytes_processed: 0,\n                docs_routed: 0,\n                last_cursor: \"delegated\".to_string(),\n                error: None,\n            };\n\n            coordinator.complete_job(job_id, &progress)?;\n        }\n\n        info!(\"Completed reshard backfill job {}\", job_id);\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Process a single reshard backfill chunk.\n    ///\n    /// This is a simulation of the full reshard backfill processing. In production,\n    /// this would:\n    /// 1. Read documents from old shard range [start_shard, end_shard)\n    /// 2. Re-route each document to new shard configuration\n    /// 3. Update progress periodically (heartbeat)\n    /// 4. Handle idempotent resume from last_cursor\n    async fn process_reshard_chunk(\n        coordinator: &ModeCCoordinator,\n        job_id: &str,\n        params: &JobParams,\n        start_shard: u32,\n        end_shard: u32,\n    ) -> Result<(u64, String)> {\n        use crate::router::{shard_for_key, assign_shard_in_group};\n        use crate::topology::{Topology, Group, NodeId};\n        use std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n\n        let old_shards = params.old_shards.unwrap_or(64);\n        let target_shards = params.target_shards.unwrap_or(128);\n        let shard_count = (end_shard - start_shard) as u64;\n\n        // Simulate processing in batches to allow for progress updates\n        let batch_shards = 4; // Process 4 shards at a time\n        let mut shards_processed = 0u32;\n        let mut docs_backfilled = 0u64;\n        let mut last_cursor = start_shard.to_string();\n\n        let start_time = Instant::now();\n        let heartbeat_interval = Duration::from_secs(5);\n\n        // Create a simple topology for routing simulation\n        let topology = Topology::new(2, 1, 3); // 2 groups, 1 RF, 3 nodes per group\n\n        while start_shard + shards_processed < end_shard {\n            let batch_end = std::cmp::min(start_shard + shards_processed + batch_shards, end_shard);\n            let batch_count = (batch_end - (start_shard + shards_processed)) as u64;\n\n            // Simulate document processing for each shard\n            // Estimate ~1000 documents per shard\n            for shard_id in (start_shard + shards_processed)..batch_end {\n                let estimated_docs = 1000u64;\n\n                for i in 0..estimated_docs {\n                    // Simulate document key\n                    let doc_key = format!(\"doc-shard{}-{}\", shard_id, i);\n\n                    // Compute old shard assignment\n                    let _old_shard = shard_for_key(&doc_key, old_shards);\n\n                    // Compute new shard assignment\n                    let new_shard = shard_for_key(&doc_key, target_shards);\n\n                    // In production, we would:\n                    // 1. Read the document from the old shard\n                    // 2. Write it to the new shard assignment\n                    // 3. Handle any conflicts or duplicates\n\n                    // Simulate routing to replica groups\n                    for group in topology.groups() {\n                        let _targets = assign_shard_in_group(new_shard, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n                        // In production, we would write to these target nodes\n                    }\n                }\n\n                docs_backfilled += estimated_docs;\n            }\n\n            shards_processed = batch_end - start_shard;\n            last_cursor = (start_shard + shards_processed as u32).to_string();\n\n            // Update progress periodically\n            let elapsed = start_time.elapsed();\n            if elapsed >= heartbeat_interval {\n                // Update progress and renew claim\n                let progress = JobProgress {\n                    bytes_processed: 0,\n                    docs_routed: docs_backfilled,\n                    last_cursor: last_cursor.clone(),\n                    error: None,\n                };\n                coordinator.update_progress(job_id, &progress, crate::mode_c_coordinator::JobState::InProgress)?;\n\n                // Renew the claim\n                let _ = coordinator.renew_claim(job_id);\n\n                // Reset the timer\n                let _ = start_time.elapsed();\n            }\n\n            // Simulate processing time\n            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n        }\n\n        Ok((docs_backfilled, last_cursor))\n    }\n}\n","numLines":613,"startLine":1,"totalLines":613}}}

Mode C work-queued chunked jobs coordinator (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Any pod can claim a queued job via compare-and-swap. The coordinator provides a queue depth metric for Horizontal Pod Autoscaler:\n16\t//!\n17\t//! ```text\n18\t//! miroir:jobs:_queued (Redis set)\n19\t//!  pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n336\t                \"failed to renew job claim - may have lost ownership\"\n337\t            );\n338\t        }\n339\t\n340\t        Ok(renewed)\n341\t    }\n342\t\n343\t    /// Update job progress.\n344\t    pub fn update_progress(\n345\t        &self,\n346\t        job_id: &str,\n347\t        progress: &JobProgress,\n348\t        state: JobState,\n349\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n350\t        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(progress)\n351\t            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n352\t\n353\t        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, state.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n354\t\n355\t        debug!(\n356\t            job_id = %job_id,\n357\t            state = %state.as_str(),\n358\t            bytes_processed = progress.bytes_processed,\n359\t            \"updated job progress\"\n360\t        );\n361\t\n362\t        Ok(())\n363\t    }\n364\t\n365\t    /// Complete a job successfully.\n366\t    pub fn complete_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress) -> Result<()> {\n367\t        self.update_progress(job_id, progress, JobState::Completed)?;\n368\t\n369\t        info!(\n370\t            job_id = %job_id,\n371\t            \"completed Mode C job\"\n372\t        );\n373\t\n374\t        Ok(())\n375\t    }\n376\t\n377\t    /// Fail a job.\n378\t    pub fn fail_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress, error: String) -> Result<()> {\n379\t        let mut failed_progress = progress.clone();\n380\t        failed_progress.error = Some(error.clone());\n381\t\n382\t        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&failed_progress)\n383\t            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n384\t\n385\t        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, JobState::Failed.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n386\t\n387\t        error!(\n388\t            job_id = %job_id,\n389\t            error = %error,\n390\t            \"failed Mode C job\"\n391\t        );\n392\t\n393\t        Ok(())\n394\t    }\n395\t\n396\t    /// Split a large job into chunks and enqueue them.\n397\t    ///\n398\t    /// Called by the first pod that picks up a large job. The original job\n399\t    /// transitions to \"delegated\" state and child chunk jobs are created.\n400\t    pub fn split_job_into_chunks(\n401\t        &self,\n402\t        job: &ClaimedJob,\n403\t        chunk_specs: Vec<JobChunk>,\n404\t    ) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n405\t        let params: JobParams = serde_json::from_str(&job.params)\n406\t            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize params: {}\", e)))?;\n407\t\n408\t        let total_chunks = chunk_specs.len() as u32;\n409\t        let mut chunk_job_ids = Vec::new();\n410\t\n411\t        // Mark the parent job as delegated (in_progress with special progress)\n412\t        let delegated_progress = JobProgress {\n413\t            bytes_processed: 0,\n414\t            docs_routed: 0,\n415\t            last_cursor: \"delegated\".to_string(),\n416\t            error: None,\n417\t        };\n418\t        self.update_progress(&job.id, &delegated_progress, JobState::InProgress)?;\n419\t\n420\t        // Create chunk jobs\n421\t        for (idx, chunk) in chunk_specs.iter().enumerate() {\n422\t            let mut chunk_params = params.clone();\n423\t            chunk_params.chunk = Some(chunk.clone());\n424\t\n425\t            let chunk_job_id = format!(\"{}-chunk-{}\", job.id, idx);\n426\t            let params_json = serde_json::to_string(&chunk_params)\n427\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize chunk params: {}\", e)))?;\n428\t            let progress = JobProgress::default();\n429\t            let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n430\t                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n431\t\n432\t            let new_job = NewJob {\n433\t                id: chunk_job_id.clone(),\n434\t                type_: job.type_.clone(),\n435\t                params: params_json,\n436\t                state: JobState::Queued.as_str().to_string(),\n437\t                progress: progress_json,\n438\t                parent_job_id: Some(job.id.clone()),\n439\t                chunk_index: Some(idx as i64),\n440\t                total_chunks: Some(total_chunks as i64),\n441\t                created_at: now_ms(),\n442\t            };\n443\t\n444\t            self.task_store.insert_job(&new_job)?;\n445\t            chunk_job_ids.push(chunk_job_id);\n446\t        }\n447\t\n448\t        info!(\n449\t            parent_job_id = %job.id,\n450\t            chunk_count = total_chunks,\n451\t            \"split job into chunks\"\n452\t        );\n453\t\n454\t        Ok(chunk_job_ids)\n455\t    }\n456\t\n457\t    /// Reclaim expired claims.\n458\t    ///\n459\t    /// Returns the number of claims reclaimed.\n460\t    /// Preserves job progress for idempotent resume.\n461\t    pub fn reclaim_expired_claims(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n462\t        let now = now_ms();\n463\t        let expired_jobs = self.task_store.list_expired_claims(now)?;\n464\t\n465\t        let mut reclaimed = 0;\n466\t        for job in expired_jobs {\n467\t            // Preserve the existing progress for idempotent resume\n468\t            // The job.progress field contains the last_cursor and other state\n469\t            // needed by the next pod to resume from where the previous pod left off\n470\t            let progress_json = job.progress.clone();\n471\t\n472\t            // Clear claim and reset to queued\n473\t            self.task_store.reclaim_job_claim(&job.id, JobState::Queued.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n474\t\n475\t            debug!(\n476\t                job_id = %job.id,\n477\t                previous_claimant = ?job.claimed_by,\n478\t                \"reclaimed expired job claim\"\n479\t            );\n480\t\n481\t            reclaimed += 1;\n482\t        }\n483\t\n484\t        if reclaimed > 0 {\n485\t            info!(\n486\t                count = reclaimed,\n487\t                \"reclaimed expired job claims\"\n488\t            );\n489\t        }\n490\t\n491\t        Ok(reclaimed)\n492\t    }\n493\t\n494\t    /// Get the queue depth (number of queued jobs).\n495\t    ///\n496\t    /// Used for HPA scaling per plan §14.4.\n497\t    pub fn queue_depth(&self) -> Result<u64> {\n498\t        self.task_store.count_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())\n499\t    }\n500\t\n501\t    /// Get job by ID.\n502\t    pub fn get_job(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<Option<JobRow>> {\n503\t        self.task_store.get_job(job_id)\n504\t    }\n505\t\n506\t    /// List all chunks for a parent job.\n507\t    pub fn list_chunks(&self, parent_job_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<JobRow>> {\n508\t self.task_store.list_jobs_by_state(state)\n514\t    }\n515\t\n516\t    /// Set the claim expiration time for a job (test helper).\n517\t    ///\n518\t    /// This allows tests to simulate time passing pub fn claim_expiring_soon(&self) -> bool {\n570\t        let now = now_ms();\n571\t        self.claim_expires_at - now < 5_000\n572\t    }\n573\t}\n574\t\n575\t/// Get current UNIX timestamp in Mode C work-queued chunked jobs coordinator (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n//!\n//! Any pod can claim a queued job via compare-and-swap. Jobs have claim TTL\n//! with heartbeats; expired claims are released for reclamation.\n//!\n//! Large jobs are split into chunks on input boundaries by the first pod\n//! that picks them up. Each chunk is an independent job with a parent reference.\n//!\n//! Applied to:\n//! - §13.9 streaming dump import — chunks on NDJSON line boundaries\n//! - §13.1 reshard backfill — partitions by shard-id range\n//!\n//! ## HPA Queue Depth Metric (plan §14.4)\n//!\n//! The coordinator provides a queue depth metric for Horizontal Pod Autoscaler:\n//!\n//! ```text\n//! miroir:jobs:_queued (Redis set)\n//! SCARD miroir:jobs:_queued = miroir_background_queue_depth\n//! ```\n//!\n//! The HPA can be configured to scale on this external metric:\n//!\n//! ```yaml\n//! metrics:\n//! - type: External\n//!   external:\n//!     metric:\n//!       name: miroir_background_queue_depth\n//!     target:\n//!       type: AverageValue\n//!       averageValue: 10\n//! ```\n//!\n//! Example HPA configuration that scales up when queue depth > 10:\n//! ```yaml\n//! apiVersion: autoscaling/v2\n//! kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler\n//! metadata:\n//!   name: miroir-worker-hpa\n//! spec:\n//!   scaleTargetRef:\n//!     apiVersion: apps/v1\n//!     kind: Deployment\n//!     name: miroir-worker\n//!   minReplicas: 2\n//!   maxReplicas: 10\n//!   metrics:\n//!   - type: External\n//!     external:\n//!       metric:\n//!         name: miroir_background_queue_depth\n//!       target:\n//!         type: AverageValue\n//!         averageValue: 10\n//! ```\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::task_store::{JobRow, NewJob, TaskStore};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn, error};\n\n/// Job states (plan §14.5 Mode C).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum JobState {\n    /// Job is queued and waiting to be claimed.\n    Queued = 0,\n    /// Job is claimed and in progress.\n    InProgress = 1,\n    /// Job completed successfully.\n    Completed = 2,\n    /// Job failed.\n    Failed = 3,\n}\n\nimpl JobState {\n    /// Parse from string.\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s {\n            \"queued\" => Some(Self::Queued),\n            \"in_progress\" => Some(Self::InProgress),\n            \"completed\" => Some(Self::Completed),\n            \"failed\" => Some(Self::Failed),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Convert to string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Queued => \"queued\",\n            Self::InProgress => \"in_progress\",\n            Self::Completed => \"completed\",\n            Self::Failed => \"failed\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Job types supported by Mode C coordinator.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum JobType {\n    /// Streaming dump import (plan §13.9).\n    DumpImport = 0,\n    /// Reshard backfill (plan §13.1).\n    ReshardBackfill = 1,\n}\n\nimpl JobType {\n    /// Parse from string.\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s {\n            \"dump_import\" => Some(Self::DumpImport),\n            \"reshard_backfill\" => Some(Self::ReshardBackfill),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Convert to string.\n    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::DumpImport => \"dump_import\",\n            Self::ReshardBackfill => \"reshard_backfill\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Job progress tracking.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobProgress {\n    /// Bytes processed so far (for dump import).\n    pub bytes_processed: u64,\n    /// Documents routed so far (for dump import).\n    pub docs_routed: u64,\n    /// Last cursor position for idempotent resume.\n    pub last_cursor: String,\n    /// Any error message.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for JobProgress {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            bytes_processed: 0,\n            docs_routed: 0,\n            last_cursor: String::new(),\n            error: None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Chunk specification for a job.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobChunk {\n    /// Chunk index (0-based).\n    pub index: u32,\n    /// Total number of chunks.\n    pub total: u32,\n    /// Starting position (cursor or byte offset).\n    pub start: String,\n    /// Ending position (cursor or byte offset).\n    pub end: String,\n    /// Estimated size in bytes.\n    pub size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\n/// Job parameters.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct JobParams {\n    /// Index UID being operated on.\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    /// Primary key field (for dump import).\n    pub primary_key: Option<String>,\n    /// Shard count (for dump import routing).\n    pub shard_count: Option<u32>,\n    /// Old shard count (for reshard backfill).\n    pub old_shards: Option<u32>,\n    /// Target shard count (for reshard backfill).\n    pub target_shards: Option<u32>,\n    /// Shadow index UID (for reshard backfill).\n    pub shadow_index: Option<String>,\n    /// Chunk specification (if this is a chunk).\n    pub chunk: Option<JobChunk>,\n    /// Source data location (for dump import).\n    pub source_url: Option<String>,\n    /// Source data size (for dump import).\n    pub source_size_bytes: Option<u64>,\n}\n\n/// Mode C job coordinator.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct ModeCCoordinator {\n    /// Task store for job persistence.\n    task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n    /// Pod ID for claiming jobs.\n    pod_id: String,\n    /// Claim TTL in milliseconds (default 30s).\n    claim_ttl_ms: i64,\n    /// Heartbeat interval in milliseconds (default 10s).\n    heartbeat_interval_ms: i64,\n    /// Default chunk size in bytes (default 256 MiB).\n    default_chunk_size_bytes: u64,\n}\n\nimpl ModeCCoordinator {\n    /// Create a new Mode C coordinator.\n    pub fn new(\n        task_store: Arc<dyn TaskStore>,\n        pod_id: String,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            task_store,\n            pod_id,\n            claim_ttl_ms: 30_000,  // 30 seconds\n            heartbeat_interval_ms: 10_000,  // 10 seconds\n            default_chunk_size_bytes: 268_435_456,  // 256 MiB\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set the claim TTL.\n    pub fn with_claim_ttl_ms(mut self, ttl_ms: i64) -> Self {\n        self.claim_ttl_ms = ttl_ms;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the heartbeat interval.\n    pub fn with_heartbeat_interval_ms(mut self, interval_ms: i64) -> Self {\n        self.heartbeat_interval_ms = interval_ms;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the default chunk size.\n    pub fn with_chunk_size_bytes(mut self, size_bytes: u64) -> Self {\n        self.default_chunk_size_bytes = size_bytes;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Get the default chunk size in bytes.\n    pub fn default_chunk_size_bytes(&self) -> u64 {\n        self.default_chunk_size_bytes\n    }\n\n    /// Enqueue a new job.\n    pub fn enqueue_job(\n        &self,\n        type_: JobType,\n        params: JobParams,\n    ) -> Result<String> {\n        let job_id = format!(\"{}-{}\", type_.as_str(), uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n        let params_json = serde_json::to_string(&params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize params: {}\", e)))?;\n        let progress = JobProgress::default();\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let new_job = NewJob {\n            id: job_id.clone(),\n            type_: type_.as_str().to_string(),\n            params: params_json,\n            state: JobState::Queued.as_str().to_string(),\n            progress: progress_json,\n            parent_job_id: None,\n            chunk_index: None,\n            total_chunks: None,\n            created_at: now_ms(),\n        };\n\n        self.task_store.insert_job(&new_job)?;\n\n        debug!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            job_type = %type_.as_str(),\n            \"enqueued new Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(job_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Try to claim a queued job.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the claimed job if successful, or None if no jobs are available.\n    pub fn claim_job(&self) -> Result<Option<ClaimedJob>> {\n        // List queued jobs\n        let queued_jobs = self.task_store.list_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())?;\n\n        if queued_jobs.is_empty() {\n            return Ok(None);\n        }\n\n        // Try to claim the first available job\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let claim_expires_at = now + self.claim_ttl_ms;\n\n        for job in queued_jobs {\n            if self.task_store.claim_job(&job.id, &self.pod_id, claim_expires_at)? {\n                // Successfully claimed\n                debug!(\n                    job_id = %job.id,\n                    pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n                    \"claimed Mode C job\"\n                );\n\n                return Ok(Some(ClaimedJob {\n                    id: job.id.clone(),\n                    type_: job.type_.clone(),\n                    params: job.params.clone(),\n                    progress: job.progress.clone(),\n                    claimed_by: self.pod_id.clone(),\n                    claim_expires_at,\n                    parent_job_id: job.parent_job_id.clone(),\n                    chunk_index: job.chunk_index,\n                    total_chunks: job.total_chunks,\n                }));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // All queued jobs were claimed by another pod\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n\n    /// Renew a job claim (heartbeat).\n    ///\n    /// Returns true if the claim was renewed, false if we lost the claim.\n    pub fn renew_claim(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let claim_expires_at = now + self.claim_ttl_ms;\n\n        let renewed = self.task_store.renew_job_claim(job_id, claim_expires_at)?;\n\n        if !renewed {\n            warn!(\n                job_id = %job_id,\n                pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n                \"failed to renew job claim - may have lost ownership\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(renewed)\n    }\n\n    /// Update job progress.\n    pub fn update_progress(\n        &self,\n        job_id: &str,\n        progress: &JobProgress,\n        state: JobState,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, state.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n        debug!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            state = %state.as_str(),\n            bytes_processed = progress.bytes_processed,\n            \"updated job progress\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Complete a job successfully.\n    pub fn complete_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress) -> Result<()> {\n        self.update_progress(job_id, progress, JobState::Completed)?;\n\n        info!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            \"completed Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Fail a job.\n    pub fn fail_job(&self, job_id: &str, progress: &JobProgress, error: String) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut failed_progress = progress.clone();\n        failed_progress.error = Some(error.clone());\n\n        let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&failed_progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        self.task_store.update_job_progress(job_id, JobState::Failed.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n        error!(\n            job_id = %job_id,\n            error = %error,\n            \"failed Mode C job\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Split a large job into chunks and enqueue them.\n    ///\n    /// Called by the first pod that picks up a large job. The original job\n    /// transitions to \"delegated\" state and child chunk jobs are created.\n    pub fn split_job_into_chunks(\n        &self,\n        job: &ClaimedJob,\n        chunk_specs: Vec<JobChunk>,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        let params: JobParams = serde_json::from_str(&job.params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize params: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let total_chunks = chunk_specs.len() as u32;\n        let mut chunk_job_ids = Vec::new();\n\n        // Mark the parent job as delegated (in_progress with special progress)\n        let delegated_progress = JobProgress {\n            bytes_processed: 0,\n            docs_routed: 0,\n            last_cursor: \"delegated\".to_string(),\n            error: None,\n        };\n        self.update_progress(&job.id, &delegated_progress, JobState::InProgress)?;\n\n        // Create chunk jobs\n        for (idx, chunk) in chunk_specs.iter().enumerate() {\n            let mut chunk_params = params.clone();\n            chunk_params.chunk = Some(chunk.clone());\n\n            let chunk_job_id = format!(\"{}-chunk-{}\", job.id, idx);\n            let params_json = serde_json::to_string(&chunk_params)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize chunk params: {}\", e)))?;\n            let progress = JobProgress::default();\n            let progress_json = serde_json::to_string(&progress)\n                .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to serialize progress: {}\", e)))?;\n\n            let new_job = NewJob {\n                id: chunk_job_id.clone(),\n                type_: job.type_.clone(),\n                params: params_json,\n                state: JobState::Queued.as_str().to_string(),\n                progress: progress_json,\n                parent_job_id: Some(job.id.clone()),\n                chunk_index: Some(idx as i64),\n                total_chunks: Some(total_chunks as i64),\n                created_at: now_ms(),\n            };\n\n            self.task_store.insert_job(&new_job)?;\n            chunk_job_ids.push(chunk_job_id);\n        }\n\n        info!(\n            parent_job_id = %job.id,\n            chunk_count = total_chunks,\n            \"split job into chunks\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(chunk_job_ids)\n    }\n\n    /// Reclaim expired claims.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the number of claims reclaimed.\n    /// Preserves job progress for idempotent resume.\n    pub fn reclaim_expired_claims(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let expired_jobs = self.task_store.list_expired_claims(now)?;\n\n        let mut reclaimed = 0;\n        for job in expired_jobs {\n            // Preserve the existing progress for idempotent resume\n            // The job.progress field contains the last_cursor and other state\n            // needed by the next pod to resume from where the previous pod left off\n            let progress_json = job.progress.clone();\n\n            // Clear claim and reset to queued\n            self.task_store.reclaim_job_claim(&job.id, JobState::Queued.as_str(), &progress_json)?;\n\n            debug!(\n                job_id = %job.id,\n                previous_claimant = ?job.claimed_by,\n                \"reclaimed expired job claim\"\n            );\n\n            reclaimed += 1;\n        }\n\n        if reclaimed > 0 {\n            info!(\n                count = reclaimed,\n                \"reclaimed expired job claims\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        Ok(reclaimed)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the queue depth (number of queued jobs).\n    ///\n    /// Used for HPA scaling per plan §14.4.\n    pub fn queue_depth(&self) -> Result<u64> {\n        self.task_store.count_jobs_by_state(JobState::Queued.as_str())\n    }\n\n    /// Get job by ID.\n    pub fn get_job(&self, job_id: &str) -> Result<Option<JobRow>> {\n        self.task_store.get_job(job_id)\n    }\n\n    /// List all chunks for a parent job.\n    pub fn list_chunks(&self, parent_job_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<JobRow>> {\n        self.task_store.list_jobs_by_parent(parent_job_id)\n    }\n\n    /// List jobs by state.\n    pub fn list_jobs_by_state(&self, state: &str) -> Result<Vec<JobRow>> {\n        self.task_store.list_jobs_by_state(state)\n    }\n\n    /// Set the claim expiration time for a job (test helper).\n    ///\n    /// This allows tests to simulate time passing without actually waiting.\n    /// WARNING: This should only be used in tests!\n    #[cfg(test)]\n    pub fn set_claim_expires_at_for_test(&self, job_id: &str, expires_at: i64) -> Result<()> {\n        self.task_store.renew_job_claim(job_id, expires_at)?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// A claimed job being processed by a pod.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClaimedJob {\n    /// Job ID.\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Job type.\n    pub type_: String,\n    /// Job parameters (JSON).\n    pub params: String,\n    /// Job progress (JSON).\n    pub progress: String,\n    /// Pod that claimed this job.\n    pub claimed_by: String,\n    /// When the claim expires (UNIX ms).\n    pub claim_expires_at: i64,\n    /// Parent job ID if this is a chunk.\n    pub parent_job_id: Option<String>,\n    /// Chunk index if this is a chunk.\n    pub chunk_index: Option<i64>,\n    /// Total chunks if this is part of a chunked job.\n    pub total_chunks: Option<i64>,\n}\n\nimpl ClaimedJob {\n    /// Parse the job parameters.\n    pub fn parse_params(&self) -> Result<JobParams> {\n        serde_json::from_str(&self.params)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize params: {}\", e)))\n    }\n\n    /// Parse the current progress.\n    pub fn parse_progress(&self) -> Result<JobProgress> {\n        serde_json::from_str(&self.progress)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::TaskStore(format!(\"failed to deserialize progress: {}\", e)))\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this is a chunk job.\n    pub fn is_chunk(&self) -> bool {\n        self.parent_job_id.is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the claim is about to expire (within 5 seconds).\n    pub fn claim_expiring_soon(&self) -> bool {\n        let now = now_ms();\n        self.claim_expires_at - now < 5_000\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\npub fn now_ms() -> i64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as i64\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::task_store::SqliteTaskStore;\n\n    fn test_coordinator() -> ModeCCoordinator {\n        let store = Arc::new(SqliteTaskStore::open_in_memory().unwrap());\n        store.migrate().unwrap();\n        ModeCCoordinator::new(store, \"test-pod\".to_string())\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_job_state_roundtrip() {\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"queued\"), Some(JobState::Queued));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"in_progress\"), Some(JobState::InProgress));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"completed\"), Some(JobState::Completed));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"failed\"), Some(JobState::Failed));\n        assert_eq!(JobState::from_str(\"unknown\"), None);\n\n        assert_eq!(JobState::Queued.as_str(), \"queued\");\n        assert_eq!(JobState::InProgress.as_str(), \"in_progress\");\n        assert_eq!(JobState::Completed.as_str(), \"completed\");\n        assert_eq!(JobState::Failed.as_str(), \"failed\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_job_type_roundtrip() {\n        assert_eq!(JobType::from_str(\"dump_import\"), Some(JobType::DumpImport));\n        assert_eq!(JobType::from_str(\"reshard_backfill\"), Some(JobType::ReshardBackfill));\n        assert_eq!(JobType::from_str(\"unknown\"), None);\n\n        assert_eq!(JobType::DumpImport.as_str(), \"dump_import\");\n        assert_eq!(JobType::ReshardBackfill.as_str(), \"reshard_backfill\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_enqueue_and_claim_job() {\n        let coord = test_coordinator();\n\n        let params = JobParams {\n            index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n            primary_key: Some(\"id\".to_string()),\n            shard_count: Some(64),\n            old_shards: None,\n            target_shards: None,\n            shadow_index: None,\n            chunk: None,\n            source_url: Some(\"https://example.com/dump.ndjson\".to_string()),\n            source_size_bytes: Some(1_000_000_000),\n        };\n\n        let job_id = coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim the job\n        let claimed = coord.claim_job().unwrap();\n        assert!(claimed.is_some());\n        let claimed = claimed.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claimed.id, job_id);\n        assert_eq!(claimed.claimed_by, \"test-pod\");\n\n        // Parse params\n        let parsed_params = claimed.parse_params().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(parsed_params.index_uid, \"test-index\");\n        assert_eq!(parsed_params.primary_key, Some(\"id\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_renewal() {\n        let coord = test_coordinator();\n\n        let params = JobParams {\n            index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n            primary_key: None,\n            shard_count: None,\n            old_shards: None,\n            target_shards: None,\n            shadow_index: None,\n            chunk: None,\n            source_url: None,\n            source_size_bytes: None,\n        };\n\n        let job_id = coord.enqueue_job(JobType::ReshardBackfill, params).unwrap();\n        let claimed = coord.claim_job().unwrap().unwrap();\n\n        // Add a small delay to ensure time advances\n        std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(10));\n\n        // Renew the claim\n        let renewed = coord.renew_claim(&job_id).unwrap();\n        assert!(renewed);\n\n        // Get the job and verify claim was extended\n        let job = coord.get_job(&job_id).unwrap().unwrap();\n        assert!(job.claim_expires_at.unwrap() > claimed.claim_expires_at);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_split_job_into_chunks() {\n        let coord = test_coordinator();\n\n        let params = JobParams {\n            index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n            primary_key: Some(\"id\".to_string()),\n            shard_count: Some(64),\n            old_shards: None,\n            target_shards: None,\n            shadow_index: None,\n            chunk: None,\n            source_url: Some(\"https://example.com/dump.ndjson\".to_string()),\n            source_size_bytes: Some(1_000_000_000),\n        };\n\n        let job_id = coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params).unwrap();\n        let claimed = coord.claim_job().unwrap().unwrap();\n\n        // Create 3 chunks\n        let chunks = vec![\n            JobChunk {\n                index: 0,\n                total: 3,\n                start: \"0\".to_string(),\n                end: \"333333333\".to_string(),\n                size_bytes: 333_333_333,\n            },\n            JobChunk {\n                index: 1,\n                total: 3,\n                start: \"333333333\".to_string(),\n                end: \"666666666\".to_string(),\n                size_bytes: 333_333_333,\n            },\n            JobChunk {\n                index: 2,\n                total: 3,\n                start: \"666666666\".to_string(),\n                end: \"1000000000\".to_string(),\n                size_bytes: 333_333_334,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        let chunk_ids = coord.split_job_into_chunks(&claimed, chunks).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(chunk_ids.len(), 3);\n\n        // Verify chunks are queued\n        let child_jobs = coord.list_chunks(&job_id).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(child_jobs.len(), 3);\n\n        for (idx, child) in child_jobs.iter().enumerate() {\n            assert_eq!(child.state, \"queued\");\n            assert_eq!(child.parent_job_id, Some(job_id.clone()));\n            assert_eq!(child.chunk_index, Some(idx as i64));\n            assert_eq!(child.total_chunks, Some(3));\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_complete_and_fail_job() {\n        let coord = test_coordinator();\n\n        let params = JobParams {\n            index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n            primary_key: None,\n            shard_count: None,\n            old_shards: None,\n            target_shards: None,\n            shadow_index: None,\n            chunk: None,\n            source_url: None,\n            source_size_bytes: None,\n        };\n\n        let job_id = coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params.clone()).unwrap();\n        let claimed = coord.claim_job().unwrap().unwrap();\n\n        // Complete the job\n        let progress = JobProgress {\n            bytes_processed: 1_000_000,\n            docs_routed: 10_000,\n            last_cursor: \"1000000\".to_string(),\n            error: None,\n        };\n        coord.complete_job(&job_id, &progress).unwrap();\n\n        let job = coord.get_job(&job_id).unwrap().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(job.state, \"completed\");\n\n        // Test failure\n        let job_id2 = coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params).unwrap();\n        let claimed2 = coord.claim_job().unwrap().unwrap();\n\n        let fail_progress = JobProgress::default();\n        coord.fail_job(&job_id2, &fail_progress, \"test error\".to_string()).unwrap();\n\n        let job2 = coord.get_job(&job_id2).unwrap().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(job2.state, \"failed\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_queue_depth() {\n        let coord = test_coordinator();\n\n        let params = JobParams {\n            index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n            primary_key: None,\n            shard_count: None,\n            old_shards: None,\n            target_shards: None,\n            shadow_index: None,\n            chunk: None,\n            source_url: None,\n            source_size_bytes: None,\n        };\n\n        assert_eq!(coord.queue_depth().unwrap(), 0);\n\n        coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params.clone()).unwrap();\n        coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params.clone()).unwrap();\n        coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params).unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(coord.queue_depth().unwrap(), 3);\n\n        // Claim one job\n        coord.claim_job().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(coord.queue_depth().unwrap(), 2);\n Reshard backfill chunking for Mode C coordinator (plan §13.1 + §14.5).\n//!\n//! Splits reshard backfill work by shard-id ranges.\n//! Each chunk can process a range of old shards independently.\n\nuse crate::mode_c_coordinator::{JobChunk, JobParams};\n\n/// Chunk specification for a reshard backfill.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ReshardChunkSpec {\n    /// Chunk index (0-based).\n    pub index: u32,\n    /// Total number of chunks.\n    pub total: u32,\n    /// Starting old shard ID (inclusive).\n    pub start_shard: u32,\n    /// Ending old shard ID (exclusive).\n    pub end_shard: u32,\n    /// Number of shards in this chunk.\n    pub shard_count: u32,\n}\n\n/// Split a reshard backfill into chunks by shard-id ranges.\n///\n/// Returns a vector of chunk specifications. Each chunk contains a range\n/// of old shards to backfill to the new shard configuration.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `old_shards` - Number of shards in the old configuration\n/// * `target_shards` - Number of shards in the new configuration\n/// * `shards_per_chunk` - Target number of old shards per chunk\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// A vector of chunk specifications\npub fn split_reshard_into_chunks(\n    old_shards: u32,\n    target_shards: u32,\n    shards_per_chunk: u32,\n) -> Vec<ReshardChunkSpec> {\n    if old_shards == 0 {\n        return Vec::new();\n    }\n\n    // If we have fewer shards than the chunk size, return a single chunk\n    if old_shards <= shards_per_chunk {\n        return vec![ReshardChunkSpec {\n            index: 0,\n            total: 1,\n            start_shard: 0,\n            end_shard: old_shards,\n            shard_count: old_shards,\n        }];\n    }\n\n    let mut chunks = Vec::new();\n    let mut current_shard = 0u32;\n    let mut chunk_index = 0u32;\n\n    while current_shard < old_shards {\n        let end_shard = (current_shard + shards_per_chunk).min(old_shards);\n        let shard_count = end_shard - current_shard;\n\n        chunks.push(ReshardChunkSpec {\n            index: chunk_index,\n            total: 0, // Will be filled in later\n            start_shard: current_shard,\n            end_shard,\n            shard_count,\n        });\n\n        current_shard = end_shard;\n        chunk_index += 1;\n    }\n\n    // Update the total count for all chunks\n    let total = chunks.len() as u32;\n    for chunk in &mut chunks {\n        chunk.total = total;\n    }\n\n    chunks\n}\n\n/// Convert reshard chunk specs to job chunks for the Mode C coordinator.\npub fn reshard_specs_to_job_chunks(specs: Vec<ReshardChunkSpec>) -> Vec<JobChunk> {\n    specs\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|spec| JobChunk {\n            index: spec.index,\n            total: spec.total,\n            start: spec.start_shard.to_string(),\n            end: spec.end_shard.to_string(),\n            size_bytes: spec.shard_count as u64, // Use shard count as the size metric\n        })\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Parse a reshard chunk from a job chunk.\n///\n/// Returns the shard range for the chunk.\npub fn parse_reshard_chunk(chunk: &JobChunk) -> Result<(u32, u32), String> {\n    let start_shard = chunk\n        .start\n        .parse::<u32>()\n        .map_err(|e| format!(\"invalid start shard: {}\", e))?;\n    let end_shard = chunk\n        .end\n        .parse::<u32>()\n        .map_err(|e| format!(\"invalid end shard: {}\", e))?;\n\n    Ok((start_shard, end_shard))\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_zero_old_shards() {\n        let chunks = split_reshard_into_chunks(0, 128, 16);\n        assert!(chunks.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_single_chunk() {\n        let chunks = split_reshard_into_chunks(16, 32, 32);\n        assert_eq!(chunks.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].index, 0);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].total, 1);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].start_shard, 0);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].end_shard, 16);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].shard_count, 16);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_multiple_chunks() {\n        let chunks = split_reshard_into_chunks(64, 128, 16);\n        assert_eq!(chunks.len(), 4);\n\n        // Verify first chunk\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].index, 0);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].total, 4);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].start_shard, 0);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].end_shard, 16);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].shard_count, 16);\n\n        // Verify second chunk\n        assert_eq!(chunks[1].index, 1);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[1].start_shard, 16);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[1].end_shard, 32);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[1].shard_count, 16);\n\n        // Verify last chunk\n        assert_eq!(chunks[3].index, 3);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[3].start_shard, 48);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[3].end_shard, 64);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[3].shard_count, 16);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_partial_final_chunk() {\n        // 65 shards with 16 per chunk = 4 full chunks + 1 partial\n        let chunks = split_reshard_into_chunks(65, 128, 16);\n        assert_eq!(chunks.len(), 5);\n\n        // First 4 chunks should have 16 shards each\n        for i in 0..4 {\n            assert_eq!(chunks[i].shard_count, 16);\n        }\n\n        // Last chunk should have 1 shard\n        assert_eq!(chunks[4].shard_count, 1);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[4].start_shard, 64);\n        assert_eq!(chunks[4].end_shard, 65);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_chunks_cover_full_range() {\n        let old_shards = 100;\n        let chunks = split_reshard_into_chunks(old_shards, 200, 15);\n\n        let mut total_shards = 0u32;\n        for chunk in &chunks {\n            total_shards += chunk.shard_count;\n        }\n\n        assert_eq!(total_shards, old_shards);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_specs_to_job_chunks() {\n        let specs = vec![\n            ReshardChunkSpec {\n                index: 0,\n                total: 2,\n                start_shard: 0,\n                end_shard: 32,\n                shard_count: 32,\n            },\n            ReshardChunkSpec {\n                index: 1,\n                total: 2,\n                start_shard: 32,\n                end_shard: 64,\n                shard_count: 32,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        let job_chunks = reshard_specs_to_job_chunks(specs);\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[0].index, 0);\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[0].total, 2);\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[0].start, \"0\");\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[0].end, \"32\");\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[1].index, 1);\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[1].start, \"32\");\n        assert_eq!(job_chunks[1].end, \"64\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_reshard_chunk() {\n        let job_chunk = JobChunk {\n            index: 0,\n            total: 1,\n            start: \"16\".to_string(),\n            end: \"32\".to_string(),\n            size_bytes: 16,\n        };\n\n        let (start, end) = parse_reshard_chunk(&job_chunk).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(start, 16);\n        assert_eq!(end, 32);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_reshard_chunk_invalid() {\n        let job_chunk = JobChunk {\n            index: 0,\n            total: 1,\n            start: \"invalid\".to_string(),\n            end: \"32\".to_string(),\n            size_bytes: 16,\n        };\n\n        assert!(parse_reshard_chunk(&job_chunk).is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_large_reshard() {\n        // Simulate resharding from 64 to 128 shards\n        let chunks = split_reshard_into_chunks(64, 128, 8);\n        assert_eq!(chunks.len(), 8);\n\n        // Verify sequential coverage\n        let mut last_end = 0;\n        for chunk in &chunks {\n            assert_eq!(chunk.start_shard, last_end);\n            assert!(chunk.end_shard > chunk.start_shard);\n            last_end = chunk.end_shard;\n        }\n\n        assert_eq!(last_end, 64);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_uneven_chunk_distribution() {\n        // 50 shards with 12 per chunk = 4 chunks (12, 12, 12, 14)\n        let chunks = split_reshard_into_chunks(50, 100, 12);\n        assert_eq!(chunks.len(), 5);\n\n        assert_eq!(chunks[0].shard_count, 12);\n }\n39\t}\n40\t\n41\t/// Create a test reshard backfill job params.\n42\tfn reshard_backfill_params(old_shards: u32, target_shards: u32) -> JobParams {\n43\t    JobParams {\n44\t        index_uid: test_acceptance_1gb_dump_splits_into_4_chunks() {\n58\t    // Acceptance: 1 GB dump: first pod splits into 4× 256 MiB chunks\n59\t    let coord = test_coordinator(\"pod-1\");\n60\t\n61\t    // Enqueue a 1GB dump import job\n62\t    let params = dump_import_params(1_073_741_824); // 1 GiB\n63\t    let job_id = coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params.clone()).unwrap();\n64\t\n65\t    // Claim the job\n66\t    let claimed = coord.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"should claim job\");\n67\t    assert_eq!(claimed.id, job_id);\n68\t    assert_eq!(claimed.claimed_by, \"pod-1\");\n69\t\n70\t    // Split into chunks (4 chunks of ~256 MiB each)\n71\t    let chunk_size = 268_435_456; // 256 MiB\n72\t    // Ceiling division: (size + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size\n73\t    let total_chunks = ((1_073_741_824 + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size) as u32;\n74\t\n75\t    let chunks: Vec<_> = (0..total_chunks)\n76\t        .map(|i| {\n77\t            let i = i as u64;\n78\t            let start = i * chunk_size;\n79\t            let end = std::cmp::min(start + chunk_size, 1_073_741_824u64);\n80\t            crate::mode_c_coordinator::JobChunk {\n81\t                index: i as u32,\n82\t                total: total_chunks,\n83\t                start: start.to_string(),\n84\t                end: end.to_string(),\n85\t                size_bytes: end - start,\n86\t            }\n87\t        })\n88\t        .collect();\n89\t\n90\t    let chunk_ids = coord.split_job_into_chunks(&claimed, chunks).unwrap();\n91\t    assert_eq!(chunk_ids.len(), 4);\n92\t\n93\t    // Verify chunks are queued\n94\t    let child_jobs = coord.list_chunks(&job_id).unwrap();\n95\t    assert_eq!(child_jobs.len(), 4);\n96\t\n97\t    // Verify all chunks are in queued state\n98\t    for child in &child_jobs {\n99\t        assert_eq!(child.state, \"queued\");\n100\t        assert_eq!(child.parent_job_id, Some(job_id.clone()));\n101\t    }\n102\t}\n103\t\n104\t#[test]\n105\tfn test_acceptance_claim_expires_after_30s() {\n106\t    // Acceptance: Kill a claimant mid-chunk: claim expires in 30s;\n107\t    // another pod picks up and resumes at last_cursor\n108\t    let store = Arc::new(SqliteTaskStore::open_in_memory().unwrap());\n109\t    store.migrate().unwrap();\n110\t\n111\t    let coord1 = test_coordinator_with_store(\"pod-1\", store.clone());\n112\t\n113\t    // Enqueue a job\n114\t    let params = dump_import_params(1_000_000_000);\n115\t    let job_id = coord1.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params).unwrap();\n116\t\n117\t    // Pod 1 claims the job\n118\t    let claimed = coord1.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"should claim job\");\n119\t    assert_eq!(claimed.claimed_by, \"pod-1\");\n120\t\n121\t    // Update progress to simulate some work done\n122\t    let progress = JobProgress {\n123\t        bytes_processed: 500_000_000,\n124\t        docs_routed: 5000,\n125\t        last_cursor: \"500000000\".to_string(),\n126\t        error: None,\n127\t    };\n128\t    coord1.update_progress(&job_id, &progress, JobState::InProgress).unwrap();\n129\t\n130\t    // Manually set the claim expiration to the past to simulate time passing\n131\t    // In a real scenario, the pod would crash and stop renewing\n132\t    let expired_time = now_ms() - 1000; // 1 second ago\n133\t    coord1.set_claim_expires_at_for_test(&job_id, expired_time).unwrap();\n134\t\n135\t    // Verify the claim is now expired\n136\t    let job = coord1.get_job(&job_id).unwrap().unwrap();\n137\t    assert!(job.claim_expires_at.unwrap() < now_ms());\n138\t\n139\t    // Create a second coordinator representing another pod with the SAME store\n140\t    let coord2 = test_coordinator_with_store(\"pod-2\", store);\n141\t\n142\t    // Reclaim expired claims\n143\t    let reclaimed = coord2.reclaim_expired_claims().unwrap();\n144\t    assert_eq!(reclaimed, 1); // Should reclaim pod-1's expired claim\n145\t\n146\t    // Verify the job is back in queued state\n147\t    let job = coord2.get_job(&job_id).unwrap().unwrap();\n148\t    assert_eq!(job.state, \"queued\");\n149\t    assert!(job.claimed_by.is_none());\n150\t\n151\t    // Pod 2 can now claim the job\n152\t    let claimed2 = coord2.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"should reclaim job\");\n153\t    assert_eq!(claimed2.id, job_id);\n154\t    assert_eq!(claimed2.claimed_by, \"pod-2\");\n155\t\n156\t    // Verify progress was preserved for idempotent resume\n157\t    let reclaimed_progress = claimed2.parse_progress().unwrap();\n158\t    assert_eq!(reclaimed_progress.last_cursor, \"500000000\");\n159\t}\n160\t\n161\t#[test]\n162\tfn test_acceptance_hpa_queue_depth_metric() {\n163\t    // Acceptance: HPA on miroir_background_queue_depth > 10 triggers scale-up\n164\t    // during the burst; scale-down once empty\n165\t    let coord = test_coordinator(\"pod-metrics\");\n166\t\n167\t    // Initially empty\n168\t    assert_eq!(coord.queue_depth().unwrap(), 0);\n169\t\n170\t    // Enqueue 15 jobs (above HPA threshold of 10)\n171\t    for i in 0..15 {\n172\t        let params = dump_import_params(1_000_000_000);\n173\t        let job_id = format!(\"job-{}\", i);\n174\t        coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params).unwrap();\n175\t    }\n176\t\n177\t    // Queue depth should be 15\n178\t    assert_eq!(coord.queue_depth().unwrap(), 15);\n179\t\n180\t    // Claim 5 jobs\n181\t    for _ in 0..5 {\n182\t        coord.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"should claim\");\n183\t    }\n184\t\n185\t    // Queue depth should now be 10 (at HPA threshold)\n186\t    assert_eq!(coord.queue_depth().unwrap(), 10);\n187\t\n188\t    // Complete the remaining jobs\n189\t    for _ in 0..5 {\n190\t        if let Some(claimed) = coord.claim_job().unwrap() {\n191\t            let progress = JobProgress::default();\n192\t            coord.complete_job(&claimed.id, &progress).unwrap();\n193\t        }\n194\t    }\n195\t\n196\t    // Queue depth should be 5\n197\t    assert_eq!(coord.queue_depth().unwrap(), 5);\n198\t\n199\t    // Claim and complete remaining jobs\n200\t    while let Some(claimed) = coord.claim_job().unwrap() {\n201\t        let progress = JobProgress::default();\n202\t        coord.complete_job(&claimed.id, &progress).unwrap();\n203\t    }\n204\t\n205\t    // Queue should be empty (scale-down condition)\n206\t    assert_eq!(coord.queue_depth().unwrap(), 0);\n207\t}\n208\t\n209\t#[test]\n210\tfn test_acceptance_two_concurrent_dumps_interleave() {\n211\t    // Acceptance: Two concurrent dumps: chunks from both interleave in claims;\n212\t    // neither starves\n213\t    let coord = test_acceptance_1gb_dump_splits_into_4_chunks() {\n    // Acceptance: 1 GB dump: first pod splits into 4× 256 MiB chunks\n    let coord = test_coordinator(\"pod-1\");\n\n    // Enqueue a 1GB dump import job\n    let params = dump_import_params(1_073_741_824); // 1 GiB\n    let job_id = coord.enqueue_job(JobType::DumpImport, params.clone()).unwrap();\n\n    // Claim the job\n    let claimed = coord.claim_job().unwrap().expect(\"should claim job\");\n    assert_eq!(claimed.id, job_id);\n    assert_eq!(claimed.claimed_by, \"pod-1\");\n\n    // Split into chunks (4 chunks of ~256 MiB each)\n    let chunk_size = 268_435_456; // 256 MiB\n    // Ceiling division: (size + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size\n    let total_chunks = ((1_073_741_824 + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size) as u32;\n\n    let 1_073_741_824u64);\n            crate::mode_c_coordinator::JobChunk {\n                index: i as u32,\n                total: total_chunks,\n                start: start.to_string(),\n
